THE CAMPAIGN PLAY RULES
                                                                                                         Marshal your command and prepare to fight
                                                                                                         any battle across the Inner Sphere! This
                                                                                                         volume focuses on the force a player will build
                                                                                                         and field through any level of play.

                                                                                                         Campaign Operations contains rules for creating
                                                                                                         and running a combat unit, from a down-on-their-
                                                                                                         luck mercenary outfit to a fully-supplied House
                                                                                                         regiment. The Formation Building rules provide
                                                                                                         tools to craft a wide variety of Inner Sphere lances
                                                                                                         and Clan Stars for any type of scenario. The volume
                                                                                                         also contains complete rules for devising solar
                                                                                                         systems to flesh out your settings.

                                                                                                         Inside, players will find several options for
                                                                                                         campaign play, including the most expansive Chaos
                                                                                                         Campaign rules published to date. Comprehensive
                                                                                                         maintenance, salvage, repair, customization, and
                                                                                                         design quirk rules bring the down-and-dirty action
                                                                                                         of a combat commander to life as each battle
                                                                                                         determines whether your campaign will end in
                                                                                                         victory or defeat!

                                                                           ADVANCED UNITS & EQUIPMENT
                                                                              Building on TechManual, Tactical Operations: Advanced Units &

ADVANCED RULES                                                             Equipment (TO:AUE) presents rules for the construction and use
                                                                           of advanced Support Vehicles, as well as advanced and prototype
                                                                           construction options and weapons for use by almost every unit.
   Beyond the standard rules, a legion of advanced rules exists,
allowing players to expand their games in any direction they desire.       STRATEGIC OPERATIONS
   In an effort to bring these rules to players in the most logical form   SitRep:
possible, the advanced rules are contained in three “staging” core         Forces in solar system.
rulebooks, each one staging up and building off of the previous            Beginning burn to planet.
rules set. Additionally, each one focuses on a particular “in-universe     Conflict expected to last weeks to achieve object.
time frame” that will allow players to easily grasp where a given
rulebook will “plug into” their existing game play.                          Strategic Operations (SO) is the second “staging” Advanced
                                                                           Rulebook. It contains advanced movement and combat
TACTICAL OPERATIONS (2 VOLUMES)                                            operations emphasizing the importance of aerospace units, while
SitRep:                                                                    extensive rules cover combat drops of numerous troop types into
Forces on-world.                                                           any situation. Complete game play and construction rules for
Conflict expected to last mere hours to achieve object.                    advanced aerospace units are also included.
ADVANCED RULES
   Tactical Operations: Advanced Rules (TO:AR) is the first in the
“staging” Advanced Rulebooks. Its focus is during game play, and
applies directly to a game as it unfolds on a world in the BattleTech

skills can be just as important as any warrior.
                                                                      resource. Players can tap into a strong and vibrant online

                                                                      community, tapping a wide selection of players for different                RECORD
and detailing of solar systems, building an opposing force to
                                                                      ideas on how best to adjudicate a particular situation.                     SHEETS
put in conflict with the players, crafting specialized scenario and

rules with Total Warfare (and subsequent Advanced Rulebooks),

                                                                                               THE STEPS OF FORCE CREATION
                 FORCE                                                                             Force creation follows four stages, with several steps in each.


                CREATION                                                                       Stage 1: Define the Force
                                                                                                 Step 1: Select Force Type
                                                                                                 Step 2: Select Force Background
   This chapter is intended to allow players to produce military
                                                                                               Stage 2: Select Force Size
Forces of almost any type, and to do so relatively quickly. Significant
                                                                                                 Step 1: Determine Basic Budget
freedom is granted in the process, requiring some restraint on the
                                                                                                 Step 2: Modify Budget
part of the players. On the other hand, the resulting Forces are
                                                                                               Stage 3: Procure Equipment and Hire Personnel
considered to be the products of Advanced Rules and thus not
                                                                                                 Step 1: Procure Equipment and Hire Personnel
suited for tournament play, so if players are happy with the resulting
                                                                                                 Step 2: Acquire Large Spacecraft
Forces, that is all that matters.
                                                                                                 Step 3: Force Specialties
   Terminology: These rules use the same terminology as Total
                                                                                                 Step 4: Leftover Funds & Post-Creation Purchases
Warfare: a “unit” refers to any mobile element that can be fielded
                                                                                                 Step 5: Determine Support Personnel
in a BattleTech game, such as a BattleMech or a tank. Meanwhile, a
                                                                                               Stage 4: Determine Operating Expenses
“Force” denotes all units of a “named” organization. For example,
                                                                                                 Step 1: Complete the TO&E
Snord’s Irregulars circa 3067 consisted of two battalions, while
                                                                                                 Step 2: Calculate Peacetime Costs
the Star League Defense Force Second Army circa 2767 consisted
of 4 Corps (6 BattleMech divisions, 17 infantry divisions
                                                                                                  In Stage 1, the controlling player selects the Force they want
and 9 independent regiments), yet both are considered a
                                                                                               from one of three types (government, mercenary, or pirate) and
“Force.” Regardless of type or size, a “Force” denotes all units
                                                                                               determines the Force’s background (era, faction or originating
a player incorporates into the organization they will create
                                                                                               faction, etc.). These decisions affect the subsequent stages.
and play.

not feel like calculating the detailed costs, the Equipment
Availability & Cost Table includes some generic unit prices
and modifiers (which are likely to be more expensive than the
exact prices) that may be used instead of the exact values.
   To simplify acquisition, the personnel associated with
                                                                                                                                                   INTRODUCTION
units (for example, MechWarriors) are acquired at the time
of purchase. This also affects availability for purposes of these
rules. (The actual equipment procurement and Force hiring
may happen at different times if the process is roleplayed                                                                                          FORCE
out.) Green troops are simply worth less than regulars, and                                                                                        CREATION
Elite troops can be worth their weight in gold—which makes
them unfortunately hard to hire. As a result, the availability
of a unit is raised or lowered by the Experience Rating of
                                                                                                                                                      FORCE
the personnel associated with it. These rules are also based
                                                                                                                                                    OPERATIONS
on the assumption of “one unit, one crew.” Hiring multiple
crews for one vehicle or allowing one crew to have multiple
vehicles is up to the discretion of the players.
   In addition to basic availability, modifiers may apply to                                                                                         FORMATION
the availability of equipment and personnel in this step.                                                                                             BUILDING

and represent the ease or difficulty of acquiring certain                      A mercenary clears out the last resistance to a captured MechBay.

                                                                             and infantry, 8,000 CB per ton for vehicles, and 15,000 CB per ton
                                                                             for fighters and large spacecraft. This cost is multiplied by the cost

ammunition-using units in the Force is one-quarter of the vehicle            date of obsolescence. Ubiquitous units (see p. 230) multiply spare
Force’s ammunition capacity. This represents ammunition spent                part cost by 0.75. These multipliers are applied to the unit’s spare
to maintain the skills of the Force. Divide a unit’s ammunition              cost sequentially. For example, a BattleMech (10,000 C-bills per ton
tonnage and cost of ammunition by 4 to get the monthly                       of spare parts base) that is both easy to repair (x0.8) and yet uses

                                                         INTRODUCTION
   Arnold now gives the 25,577,896 CB left in Stage 3,
Step 3 a good look. He can certainly stockpile a good
amount of ammunition and spare parts, and perhaps
look through Technical Readout: Vehicle Annex for         FORCE
some jeeps and other utility vehicles. Or he can leave   CREATION
it as a cash reserve for the Force’s lean times.

   Hannah’s mercs are not close to denting their
                                                            FORCE
unspent cash reserves with monthly expenses. Again,
                                                          OPERATIONS
fuel is a large expense, but entirely for hydrogen.
Otherwise, ammunition is her greatest cost, and
entirely from her DropShip—perhaps the GM will be
friendly and allow some energy-only modifications          FORMATION
to the Union, which would cut out half her costs. Even      BUILDING
with no cost reductions, her cash reserve would last
several years. Maybe the campaign could start with

a year-long sabbatical on Mizar.

                                                                                                Additional Rules: This section covers other rules applicable to
            FORCE                                                                            running a Force, such as developing specialties.
                                                                                                Terminology: These rules use the same terminology as Total
          OPERATIONS                                                                         Warfare: a “unit” refers to any mobile element that can be fielded
                                                                                             in a BattleTech game. Meanwhile, a “Force” denotes all units of a
                                                                                             “named” organization. For example, Snord’s Irregulars circa 3067
   This section details operation issues players may encounter as
                                                                                             consisted of 2 battalions, while the Star League Defense Force Second
they take a Force they have created (see Force Creation, p. 8) through
                                                                                             Army circa 2767 consisted of 4 corps (6 BattleMech divisions, 17 infantry
a campaign. These include:
                                                                                             divisions and 9 independent regiments), yet both are considered a
   Reputation: This section supports future hiring and campaign
                                                                                             “Force.” Regardless of type or size, a “Force” denotes all units a player
choices. While mercenaries have formal quality rankings for
                                                                                             incorporates into the organization they will create and play.
their Forces, these rules allow similar rankings to be applied to
government and pirate Forces so players can determine their

government support and employability, respectively. Reputation
can rise and fall during campaigns.

                                                                                             STAGE 1: CONTRACT OFFERS
               HIRING AND                                                                                      The term “offer” refers to any opportunity for a mission or series

               CAMPAIGNS
                                                                                                           of missions, a holdover from Force operation rules generally
                                                                                                           meant for mercenary Forces. In the case of pirates, the “offer”
                                                                                                           is actually an opportunity for action identified by the pirates’
   Once the Force’s Reputation is determined, the Force may seek                                           own information gathering efforts. Within the game universe,
employment. The general process for this is:                                                               government Forces generally aren’t given options about their
                                                                                                           assignments but, as a convenience for gaming, the controlling
   Stage 1: Find the number of offers                                                                      player may still roll up a series of offers to improve their chance
   Stage 2: Determine the employer behind each offer                                                       of getting an entertaining mission. How offers are generated
   Stage 3: Determine the type of contract                                                                 differs slightly by Force type.
   Stage 4: Negotiate terms                                                                                    Mercenaries: Mercs (and pirates seeking mercenary-style
                                                                                                           employment) seek offers on a monthly basis, with each month
   Though pirates do not have employers and government units                                               generating a different number of offers (and sometimes none). Time
have guaranteed employment, the above four steps often apply                                               spent between contracts must be tracked, as Forces have salaries
to all Force types.                                                                                        to pay, parts to replace, and ammo to use in training regardless
   For groups not incorporating A Time of War play into their                                              of employment status (see Step 2: Calculate Peacetime Operating
campaigns, note that the base target number for Negotiation                                                Costs, p. 28).
Checks is 8 on 2D6.                                                                                            Hunting for offers is generally done on hiring worlds (or
                                                                                                                                        “Hiring Halls”). The entire Force need
                                                                                                                                        not journey to a hiring world, only the
                                                                                                                                        Force’s negotiators. However, Forces
                                                                                                                                        between contracts generally find hiring
                                                                                                                                        worlds more tolerant of large numbers
                                                                                                                                        of heavily armed personnel than
                                                                                                                                        other planets.
                                                                                                                                            To determine how many Contract
                                                                                                                                        Offers are available, make 1 Negotiation
                                                                                                                                        Skill Check each month. To this roll,
                                                                                                                                        apply the modifiers applicable to
                                                                                                                                        the Negotiation Skill, and also those
                                                                                                                                        applicable to both the Hiring Hall world
                                                                                                                                        (if any) as well as the Force’s Reputation
                                                                                                                                        Modifier. Then compare the Margin of
                                                                                                                                        Success (or Failure) to the Contract Offers
                                                                                                                                        Table on page 39.
                                                                                                                                            The Hiring Hall portion of the table is a
                                                                                                                                        generic listing that differs from prior Force
                                                                                                                                        creation rules because these rules are
                                                                                                                                        meant to address any era of game play,
                                                                                                                                        and there has been high turnover in Hir-
                                                                                                                                        ing Halls in the thirty-first century alone.
                                                                                                                                        “Questionable” Hiring Halls are the likes
                                                                                                                                        of Antallos (Port Krin) and Astrokaszy;
                                                                                                                                        minor halls are worlds such as Fletcher,
                                                                                                                                        Herotitus, and Solaris VII; standard halls
                                                                                                                                        are those like Arc Royal; and great halls
                                                                                                                                        are on par with 3025-era Galatea and
                                                                                                                                        3060-era Outreach. In many eras, there
                                                                                                                                        are no examples of Hiring Halls, so players
                                                                                                                                        will need to improvise.
                                                                                                                                            Pirates: Pirates, as noted above, do not
                                                                                                                                        seek offers per se. Instead, they are look-
                                                                                                                                        ing for raiding opportunities to make up
                                                                                                                                        any shortfalls of spare parts, ammunition,
                                                                                                                                        salaries, even food and water.
              The choice of a world to garrison, or attack, can be the deciding factor of a unit’s future success.

                                                                                                                                                  43
profit from jumping as often as possible with as many DropShips as
possible, and thus will charge a hiring Force for any unused docking
collars. JumpShips will also charge for all docking collars at the rate of
100,000 CB per week per docking collar when kept idle, waiting for a
military Force to complete its mission.
  These charges may also be used as price guidelines for a military Force with
excess transportation capacity looking for profit on the side.

       Wilma’s Wabid Wombats are a company-sized combined
     arms Force hired on a “short and simple” (so the employer
     says) raid on a star system one jump from the Wombats’ current
     garrison. Lacking a DropShip or JumpShip, the Wombats
     have to contract both. The mission is short: a week to reach
     the JumpShip, the instantaneous jump, a week to the target
     planet, two weeks of raiding mayhem, then a week back to
     the JumpShip, and finally a week to the garrison planet. To
     Wilma’s delight, she finds another military Force on her garrison
     planet (Arnold’s Avengers, or something silly like that) who are
     currently on R&R and are willing to loan the Wombats their idle
     Seeker and Invader.
       The mission requires six weeks of use of the Seeker-class
     DropShip (crew of average skill) for the round trip, so
     Arnold bills the Wombats for 1.5 months at 0.5 percent
     of the Seeker’s 225,829,968 CB purchase price: 1,693,725
     CB. The Wombats are conducting the raid alone and the
     JumpShip is in use for two jumps over six weeks (from
     the time the Seeker launches to the time it lands and
     the Invader is recharged to take on a new client
     DropShip). Arnold accordingly bills the Wombats for
     three docking collars on two jumps (1,200,000 CB), plus
     four weeks of enforced idleness while the Wombats
     are transiting the target system and raiding the
     planet (1,200,000 CB). The total transport bill
     handed to the Wombats is 3,093,725 CB, which
     Wilma will carry to her employer in negotiations.

Support Payment
   Support denotes how much of the Force’s
combat operations costs the employer will
reimburse above and beyond peacetime
operating costs. Support costs include the cost
of replacement parts and materiel as well as outside
technical assistance. Customarily, employers offer either
straight support or battle-loss compensation. The level of
support is rolled from the Supplemental Contract Terms Table
after the negotiation roll.
   Under a straight support arrangement, the employer agrees
to pay a percentage of the command’s monthly peacetime
operating expenses as a bonus for the period of the mission
(not counting transport time). When combat is expected,
a straight support arrangement usually benefits the
employer, as the expenses of repairing battle damage can
quickly exceed normal maintenance costs. On cadre missions
and other light duty, such as garrison posts in “safe” zones, the
arrangement usually benefits the mercenary Force.
   With a battle-loss compensation arrangement, the                     Under liaison command, the mercenary Force accepts
employer agrees to cover a percentage of the cost of repairing       a liaison that represents the employer and accompanies
or replacing the command’s battlefield losses and damage.            them during the mission. In exchange, the employer
Customarily, the employer is required to compensate the              assumes limited legal responsibility for the actions of the
Force within six months of the date of loss. This compensation       mercenary Force during the length of the contract. The
                                                                                                                                         INTRODUCTION
is cumulative, so if a particular piece of equipment is destroyed    liaison—usually a regular officer, or mercenary veteran—
and replaced four times over the course of several battles,          monitors all mercenary command decisions and generally
the employer might owe a percentage of four replacements.            watches out for the employer’s interests. Depending on the
Compensation can be paid in C-bills or a combination of C-bills      power of the employer and the strength of the contract, a              FORCE
and equipment, but a replacement item must exactly match             liaison can bring heavy pressure to bear on the mercenary             CREATION
the destroyed item.                                                  Force and can be a damaging witness in any post-mission

breach of contract and exposes an employer to severe fines.          states favor liaison command. However, most Great Houses

or controlling toward mercenary commands. If that can’t be                                                                                MAINTENANCE,
determined, use a typical command per the Master Contract            Salvage Rights                                                         SALVAGE,
Terms Table.                                                            Throughout the Succession Wars and especially in the                REPAIR &
   Independent command allows a Force full battlefield               Clan Invasion era, salvage rights were one of the most closely      CUSTOMIZATION
autonomy, with no interference from the employer, the                contested contract terms in a mercenary negotiation. Forces
employer’s troops or an employer’s representatives (though the       have historically preferred to keep whatever they capture, as          RECORD
mercenary Force remains bound to explain any questionable            battlefield salvage can dramatically increase the profit margins       SHEETS
actions to the employer and/or any outside authority or              for a successful mission. For many mercenaries and pirate Force,
arbitrator after the contract expires). Particularly during covert   salvage is the only way to earn a profit.
missions, employers grant independent command rights                    Employers, on the other hand, see salvage rights as a way to
because doing so shields them from any legal responsibility          gain back some of the money they are spending on the Force,
for the mercenaries’ actions under the contract.                     to say nothing of the potential to reverse-engineer captured



                                                                                                                                             45
enemy technologies. The method of assigning salvage rights has
varied over the centuries, but currently four main levels of salvage              ADDITIONAL
                                                                                    RULES
rights are recognized by virtually every Force and employer in the
business: full, exchange, shared and none.
   Salvage rights apply to mercenary and government Forces,
not pirates. For mercenaries, the reasons to battle for control of       The following miscellaneous rules cover contract breaches,
salvage are obvious. Government Forces likewise have issues with       building bases, and procuring equipment after unit creation.
salvage: their commanding government may take salvage away
from a victorious Force to share with others. Pirates automatically    WARTIME DEFINITION AND COSTS
have full salvage rights.                                                 These rules use a simple, binary definition of peacetime and
   Full salvage rights and no salvage rights are self-explanatory.     wartime. A Force is considered to be in peacetime while executing
In the former case, Forces can cart off all equipment captured         certain types of missions (garrison duty, cadre duty, and security
during the mission, barring any objects or persons specially           duty), being transported to a new mission, or while unemployed.
designated by the contract (such as the targets of an extraction       In other circumstances, the Force is in wartime.
or objective raid). Gaining full salvage rights generally takes some      Wartime operating costs start at peacetime costs (for salaries,
hard bargaining, however.                                              fuel, ammunition, and spare parts), representing the assumption
   No salvage rights means the employer is entitled to all salvage     that peacetime costs are a baseline for the Force’s activity on a
the mercenary Force brings back. Employers rarely stipulate “no        mission, including regular training outside of actual combat.
salvage rights” in the absence of at least liaison command, as         Additional costs are applied according to actual combat.
the employer knows that a mercenary Force will not likely bring           Salaries will change as personnel are killed or hired. As a
anything home if it isn’t entitled to keep anything.                   simplification, the dead personnel will still be paid in full for the
   A command with exchange rights must turn over all salvaged          month in which they die.
items to its employer at the end of the mission, and in exchange          Fuel consumption starts at the peacetime baseline. Units that
receives fair market value for a percentage of the items, paid         track fuel points as a function of movement (spacecraft and
in C-bills or materiel. Exchange rights came into widespread           fighters; see pp. 32-33, SO) add fuel expended in combat missions
practice to address salvaged Clan technology, and employers            to the peacetime baseline. Other units determine the mass of fuel
customarily provide an equivalent item of advanced Inner               expended in addition to the peacetime baseline (see p. 33, SO).
Sphere technology when possible. As an ideal middle ground,               Maintenance costs add repair and replacement costs above the
most employers are willing to settle for exchange rights when          peacetime baseline. The cost to replace a component in a unit is
it comes to salvage, as the employer generally gets first pick of      equal to the price listed on pages 274-286, TM. Component repair
the equipment to be exchanged.                                         costs are one-fifth of the component purchase cost.
   Shared rights are an alternative to exchange rights that are           Ammunition costs also start at the peacetime baseline. Additional
more common in the case of missions not directed against the           combat costs are simple: track ammunition expended in combat.
Clans. Under this arrangement, the mercenary command keeps
a certain percentage of all salvaged items (figured by tonnage),       CONTRACT BREACHES AND MISSION FAILURES
while the employer receives the rest. Because the mercenaries             Sometimes, regardless of the players’ best efforts, a military Force
select the items they keep in this case, most employers prefer         fails to complete a mission or perhaps even violates the terms of
exchange rights.                                                       its contract, resulting in a breach. Players with mercenary Forces
   With regard to the Contract Terms tables for salvage rights,        should avoid events like these whenever possible, as their outcome
the player may need to make two rolls. If the roll result yields a     can seriously harm a mercenary command’s standing (see Combat
percentage or Full, the mercenary or government command is             Record Rating, p. 35).
offered that percentage of what are considered shared salvage             Pirates are exempt from contract breaches and mission failures
rights, meaning that the Force can keep that percentage of the         (unless operating under contract to an outside employer). A pirate
total monetary value of any equipment they capture during the          Force that fails its mission may, at the players’ or GM’s discretion,
contract. (The remaining percentage goes to the employer.) Full        face the wrath of its commander or Bandit King.
salvage means the mercenary command claims all captured                   Finally, government Forces suffer negative results from mission
equipment during the contract. On a result of Exchange, the            failure and contract breach just like mercenary Forces, though the
player must make a second roll, throwing out any result that           terms for contract breach are different (dereliction of duty and
does not yield a percentage or Full. The result kept is the amount     disobeying orders being common examples). The final results also
of all captured equipment for which a Force may receive fair           differ. Whereas a mercenary Force will lose Reputation and may
market value—in C-bills or equipment of equivalent (but                be fined and/or face hiring bans, a government Force usually sees
generally lower) technology. The remaining equipment goes              punishment of its personnel (such as court-martials of its officers,
to the employer.                                                       who will then likely be removed from the Force).

                     The Fourth Davion Guards stand ready to assault the highly contested Castle Brian, Glæsisvellir, on the planet Tyrfing during the First Succession War.

                                                                                                       • BattleForce: An organization of units within a Force.
            FORMATION                                                                                  • Alpha Strike: The organization of units within a player’s Force;
                                                                                                         i.e. for an Inner Sphere Force, lances of 4 units, which combine
             BUILDING                                                                                    into companies of 3 lances, and so on.
                                                                                                       • Strategic BattleForce/ACS (Inner Sphere at War): The
                                                                                                         individual playing pieces of these games represent a
   Though the rules in this section can be used in conjunction with
                                                                                                         Formation made up of one or more units, and are generally
those in Force Creation (see p. 8), they are primarily focused on
                                                                                                         regimental size.
building an effective force for a particular scenario. These rules
                                                                                                       • Formation Type: This term is used here to define a basic
expand on the guidelines for creating a scenario presented in Total
                                                                                                         grouping of units defined by their capabilities and intended
Warfare—rather than rolling random units, players can identify
                                                                                                         combat roles: i.e. a Battle Lance, an Assault Star, and so on.
each unit’s Role and learn how to overlap them to create the most
effective fighting force possible.

                                                                                                       FORMATION
   Formation: The term “formation” is used in various ways across
the BattleTech rulebooks:

                                                                                                     BUILDING BASICS
   • Force Composition/Force Structure/Formation Structure:
     All of these terms are used interchangeably to refer to the
     military organizations employed by various factions: i.e. four
     units in an Inner Sphere lance, five units in a Clan Star, six units                              Units (’Mechs, vehicles, battle armor squads, and others) are
     in a ComStar Level I, and so on.                                                               organized into Formations (lances, Stars, and Level IIs) that together
   • Un-capitalized “formation”: This term is often used when                                       comprise the player’s Force. Each unit has a Role which reflects that
     describing a large grouping of units, such as the Attacks                                      unit’s battlefield specialization. The player organizes their units into
     Against Large Infantry Formations rules (see p. 82, TO:AR), or                                 Formations, each with a Formation Type reflecting its specialization.
     the Fighter Squadrons rules (see p. 25, SO).                                                   Roles are the easiest guide to placing units into Formation Types,
   • Formation: When capitalized, Formation has slightly different                                  but not the only way. The Formation Type may bestow Special
     definitions—though all of a theme—based upon the rules                                         Pilot Abilities to some or all of the units. Furthermore, the overall
     set in question:                                                                               Experience Rating of the Force (based on the average Skill Ratings
or Piloting and Gunnery Skills of its units, as applicable) may
entitle it to Special Command Abilities. Each step is presented
below.
                                                                                   UNIT ROLE
                                                                         A Unit Role is the primary intended use of a unit on a battlefield.

  Special Command Abilities (see p. 83) apply to the overall                                                                                   CUSTOMIZATION
Force based on its Experience Rating. They reflect the preferred
tactics and specialization training of a Combat Command. The
better trained a Force, the more Special Command Abilities the                                                                                     RECORD
                                                                                                                                                   SHEETS
Force can select.
  Roles for canon units, as well as their Battle Values, can be
found on the online Master Unit List (www.MasterUnitList.info).




                                                                                                                                                    57
Missile Boat                                                              Dogfighter
   Missile Boats are similar to Snipers, but the majority of their          Dogfighters are medium or heavy fighters equipped for extended
long-range damage comes from weapons capable of indirect                  toe-to-toe engagements with fighters of comparable performance
fire, long-range missiles, mortars or artillery. Missile Boats are        and payload. Meant for the long haul, many dogfighters typically
often grouped together in order to optimize their ability to fire         feature energy-heavy armament and good heat-dissipation.
indirectly without singling out part of the Formation as either
incapable of firing, or being the only unit in the Formation left to      Fast Dogfighter
take all of the return fire. The Archer and LRM Carrier are examples        Fast Dogfighters are typically light or medium fighters. These
of Missile Boats.                                                         units are intended to act as “second shell interceptors,” able to
                                                                          engage anything that gets past the normal Interceptors, or to assist
Scout                                                                     and support dedicated Interceptors once battle is joined.
    Scouts are fast, with light armor—or none at all—and very
little weaponry. They often employ advanced electronics. Scouts           Fire Support
are sometimes forced into combat by necessity, but are more                  Fire Support craft are usually medium or heavy fighters equipped
commonly spotters or support for other units in the Force. The            with long-range weapons. These units are intended to keep up with
Raven and Swift Wind Scout Car are examples of Scouts.                    dogfighters en route to the battle, and contribute meaningful amounts
                                                                          of firepower at stand-off distances. Because many fire support units
Skirmisher                                                                are also heavy fighters with bomb-capable hard points, these units are
   Skirmishers are fast, with adequate or even heavy armor. Their         often conscripted for heavy ground support missions as well.
flexibility to rely on both speed and armor for defense can give
Skirmishers the ability to take a great deal of incoming fire, but        Interceptor
they usually prefer not to have to sit still to hold an objective or to      Interceptors are light, fast fighters intended to engage the enemy
deliver accurate fire. Skirmishers are often commanders of lighter        at the furthest possible distance from friendly assets, cause early
Strikers, can provide a flanking threat when paired with slower           attrition, and ideally hold the enemy at that maximum separation
Brawlers or Juggernauts, or are massed with other Skirmishers as          for as long as possible.
a heavy cavalry formation. The Wolverine and Dragon are examples
of Skirmishers.                                                           Transport
                                                                             Transports are airborne and aerospace units that carry units,
Sniper                                                                    troops, or materiel to or from the battlefield. Unlike the other
   Snipers prefer to find a good “sniper’s nest,” sit still and fire      aerospace Unit Roles, Transports are generally not fighters, but
at long range. Some Snipers rely on range, stealth or cover for           rather Small Craft, DropShips, and other large airborne units such
defense, while others rely on armor. Few rely on speed, as excessive      as Airship and Fixed-Wing support vehicles.
movement is detrimental to their ability to hit at long range, though
some Snipers intended to provide fire support to faster units may
have enough speed to keep up with them. The Zeus and Schrek
PPC Carrier are examples of Snipers.                                           LANCES, STARS
Striker
                                                                               AND LEVEL IIS
  Strikers are fast, with light armor, and usually prefer short- or          For the purposes of Force organization, players will want to be
medium-range engagements. They rely on their speed (or numbers)           familiar with the smallest combat Formations used by the various
to survive while threatening the rear or flanks of the enemy. The         factions of the BattleTech setting. Depending on their general
Jenner and Harasser Missile Platform are examples of Strikers.            affiliation—Inner Sphere and Periphery, Clan, or ComStar/Word
                                                                          of Blake—these are the lance, the Star, or the Level II, respectively.
AEROSPACE UNIT ROLES                                                      From the perspective of the factions that employ them, these
  Even more so than with ground units, speed is often the defining        Formations represent the building blocks of larger Forces.
factor in an aerospace unit’s Role. The preferred range of slower            The various lance, Star, and Level II Formation Types are explained
units differentiates them into separate Roles.                            below. A summary of these groups and the larger Formations they
                                                                          build appears in the Standard Force Organization Schemes Table
Attack Fighter                                                            on page 59.
  Attack Fighters are usually medium or heavy fighters that mount            Non-Infantry Ground Units: Among the major realms of the
heavy firepower at the expense of maneuverability and/or armor.           Inner Sphere and the Periphery, the lance is smallest ground
These units pack heavier weapons to achieve this punch, which             Formation in use. A lance consists of four BattleMechs or combat
tends to leave them somewhat slower and lacking in long-range             vehicles, commanded by a lieutenant. The Clan equivalent of this
power compared to other fighters. These features make them                Formation, the Star, is composed of five ’Mechs or 10 combat
particularly effective in air-to-ground attack missions.                  vehicles, led by a Star Commander. Finally, ComStar and the Word
                                                                          of Blake use a six-unit group known as a Level II. These “ComStar
                                                                          lances” are generally commanded by an Adept.

conventional fighters.                                                           use the ability must still meet its requisites. For example, a unit in

six ’Mechs, vehicles, infantry or aerospace fighters, though fighters                While the bonus abilities provided here are fairly balanced

   Interceptor Squadrons are fast aerospace combat groups meant                        COMPANIES,
to deal with opposing aerospace threats before they get too close.
Because more emphasis is placed on speed, these squadrons often                       BINARIES, AND
do not have the firepower or armor for heavy fighting. Instead, their
goal is to deliver the first strike, in an effort to sow chaos in the enemy’s            BEYOND
ranks or possibly buy time for the heavier fighters to enter the fray.

                                                                                                            Not all SPAs are created equal, however, so a point value has
       SPECIAL PILOT                                                                                     also been assigned to these abilities to reflect their complexity

         ABILITIES
                                                                                                         and effectiveness in battle. Low-level warriors and crews—those
                                                                                                         identified as Green, Very Green, or Wet Behind the Ears—should
                                                                                                         receive no special pilot abilities. Middle-grade units—those with a
   In the BattleTech universe, opposing forces sometimes clash at the                                    Regular Skill Rating—may receive one special pilot abilities with a
size of armies, and troops often share the same combat experiences,                                      point value of 2 or less. Experienced units—those rated as Veteran
but it remains possible for superlative warriors to emerge and set                                       or Elite—may receive up to two different special pilot abilities with
themselves apart from the crowd. The abilities possessed by these                                        a combined point value of 4 or less. Truly superior units—those
special warriors represent a unique combination of experience,                                           with a Skill Rating of Heroic or Legendary—may receive up to
talent, and personal traits that can vary from individual to individual.                                 three different special pilot abilities, with a combined point value
   For this reason, unless otherwise indicated, special pilot abilities                                  of 6 or less.
(SPAs) are assumed to apply to a single warrior, vehicle crew, or                                           Finally, unless the players are making use of the Formation Types
infantry unit as appropriate, rather than to an entire Force. These                                      rules and their associated Bonus Special Pilot Abilities (see pp.
abilities will augment the warrior or crew’s unit, and can “move”                                        60-68), the total number of warriors or crews that receive special
with them between machines—a particularly useful feature if                                              pilot abilities should also be limited, to avoid over-complicating
the players are involved in a campaign where salvage and unit                                            the game at hand. If not using the Formation Types rules, the rule
replacements are common.                                                                                 of thumb is that a player’s Force should try to avoid assigning SPAs
                                                                                                         to more than 1 unit for every 4 units fielded in the scenario. These
ASSIGNING SPECIAL PILOT ABILITIES                                                                        special pilots may be part of the same Formation, or spread out
   If the players are interested in running a BattleTech game which                                      among several Formations, as the controlling player sees fit.
includes special pilot abilities, but without any of the notable                                            If the players are using the Formation Types rules, the total
warriors found in published sources, they may choose to purchase                                         number of warriors or crews that receive Special Pilot Abilities
such abilities from the list below, based on the individual unit’s Skill                                 should be limited to 1 unit for every 12 units fielded in the scenario.
Rating, and the type of unit they control.                                                                  All SPAs presented below are designed to modify already-
                                                                                                         existing features of the units that the pilot or crew is operating,
                                                                                                                                  but will not fundamentally alter the unit’s
                                                                                                                                  normal capabilities unless specifically noted
                                                                                                                                  otherwise. Thus, they cannot be applied to
                                                                                                                                  pilots or crews of unit types other than those
                                                                                                                                  listed in their descriptions.
                                                                                                                                     This means that SPAs that modify damage
                                                                                                                                  for a specific type of attack—such as the
                                                                                                                                  Swordsman SPA’s focus on units which possess
                                                                                                                                  a physical weapon—require that the unit
                                                                                                                                  actually possess that type of attack in order
                                                                                                                                  to make use of the pilot’s talent. If the unit
                                                                                                                                  cannot ordinarily meet that requirement—
                                                                                                                                  such as a Swordsman who finds himself
                                                                                                                                  operating a ’Mech that lacks any physical
                                                                                                                                  weapon—the SPA will simply not apply, as
                                                                                                                                  there is nothing for it to modify.
                                                                                                                                     This same principle also applies to special
                                                                                                                                  pilot abilities that modify movement, or which
                                                                                                                                  do not list the unit in question among the unit
                                                                                                                                  types for which the ability applies. Thus, a
                                                                                                                                  special pilot ability designated for ’Mech units
                                                                                                                                  only cannot be used with a vehicle unit type.
                                                                                                                                     Furthermore, movement modifications that
                                                                                                                                  do not apply to the unit, such as abilities that
                                                                                                                                  might modify wheeled ground movement on
                                                                                                                                  a vehicle unit that has a tracked motive type,
                                                                                                                                  cannot be granted, even if the special pilot
                                                                                                                                  ability is otherwise compatible with the unit.
                                                                                                                                  All terrain restrictions that affect the unit type
 B1BXX
                                                                                                                                  itself will remain in effect unless the special
  Two desperate MechWarriors have drawn on every ounce of their skill and tenacity to reach the conclusion of this gritty battle. ability notes otherwise.

Cluster Hitter              2     Unit can deliver extra damage in an attack using missiles or flak weapons                              FORCE
                                                                                                                                        CREATION

Demoralizer                 3     Unit can intimidate an opponent for a brief period                                                     FORCE
                                                                                                                                       OPERATIONS
Dodge                       2     Unit can attempt to evade physical attacks
Eagle’s Eyes                2     Unit gains (or augments) its ability to spot hidden units and avoid mines

                                                                                                                                      BUILDING

Forward Observer            1     Unit improves accuracy of indirect fire when used as a spotter

                                                                                                                                      INNER SPHERE

                                                                                                                                         AT WAR

Marksman                    2     Unit may make an Aimed Shot
Melee Master                2     Unit can deliver an additional physical attack

Melee Specialist            1     Unit delivers physical attacks with greater accuracy                                                 CAMPAIGNS
Multi-Tasker                2     Reduces penalty modifiers for attacks against multiple targets
Natural Grace               3     Reduces MP costs through certain terrain, enables improved movement abilities

Oblique Artilleryman        1     Improves accuracy and reduces scatter for all artillery weapon attacks

Range Master                2     Unit gains bonus to attacks at preferred range
                                                                                                                                         CHAOS
Sandblaster                 2     Unit improves accuracy and damage when only using AC and missile weapons                              CAMPAIGN
Sharpshooter                4     Unit may make an Aimed Shot with additional chance for critical hit                                   CREATION

Slugger                     1     Unit may fire weapon while using an improvised club                                                 MAINTENANCE,
Sniper                      3     Unit reduces Medium, Long, and Extreme range modifiers by half                                        SALVAGE,

Street Fighter              2     Unit may pre-empt an attack against it by enemies in base contact                                      RECORD

       Ability                           Cost     Brief Description
       Terrain Master
          Drag Racer                      3       Ground vehicle unit gains extra speed on ice or pavement; avoids skidding better
          Forest Ranger                   3       Unit moves more easily through (and gains extra cover from) woods and jungle
          Frogman                         3       Unit moves more easily than others while fully submerged
          Mountaineer                     3       Unit moves more easily through Level changes and rough terrain types
          Nightwalker                     3       Unit ignores modifiers for darkness
          Swamp Beast                     3       Unit moves more easily through mud and swamp terrain; ignores bog down in same
       Weapon Specialist                  3       Unit can deliver a more accurate attack with preferred weapon
       Zweihander                         2       ’Mech unit delivers more damage in physical attacks
       Airborne Only Abilities           Cost     Brief Description
       Dust-Off                           2       Enables airborne unit types to land or liftoff in non-clear terrain
       Golden Goose                       3       Improves accuracy for air-to-ground strafing, strike, and bombing attacks
       Ground-Hugger                      2       Airborne unit may execute a double-strafe or double-strike air-to-ground attack
       Ride the Wash                      4       Unit reduces atmospheric combat modifiers; may execute special air-to-air attack
       Shaky Stick                        2       Airborne unit is harder to hit from the ground during air-to-ground attacks
       Wind Walker                        2       Unit ignores atmospheric combat modifiers and gains a bonus to landing and liftoff
       Infantry Only Abilities           Cost     Brief Description
       Foot Cavalry                       1       Foot-based infantry unit moves faster, even through difficult terrain
       Heavy Horse                        2       Beast-mounted infantry unit can inflict extra damage at point-blank range
       Light Horseman                     2       Beast-mounted infantry unit moves faster, even through difficult terrain
       Urban Guerrilla                    1       Infantry unit is harder to attack in urban terrain, and may “spawn” support



ANIMAL MIMICRY                                                                • The disturbingly realistic “animalisms” of the ’Mech’s movement
   “If you think the bark’s bad, wait till you feel my bite…”                   adds a +1 modifier to any Morale Checks (see pp. 179-181,
   Unit Type: See below                                                         TO:AR)—or a –1 target modifier to the Demoralizer Piloting
   SPA Cost: 2 points                                                           Skill Roll if using the Demoralizer SPA (see p. 74).
   A pilot with the Animal Mimicry Ability has combined an
exceptional understanding of animal behavior with his natural
aptitude for ’Mech piloting to give his machine the uncanny—
some would even say frighteningly— resemblance to a wild animal.
Animal Mimicry is available only to pilots of Quad BattleMechs
and ProtoMechs, and of ’Mech and ProtoMech designs that, by
agreement of all players, feature an animal look to them—such as
beastly Clan totem ’Mechs like the Kodiak and the Mandrill.
   Like Natural Grace (see p. 78), Animal Mimicry is open to
interpretation during gameplay, but can grant the following
additional capabilities:
   • The superior, naturally inspired gait provides a –1 target
       modifier to all Piloting Skill Rolls required for Quad designs.
   • The animal-like flexibility, communicated into the ’Mech’s
       movements, enables it to navigate wooded terrain at a cost
       of –1 MP per hex of light, heavy, or ultraheavy woods and
       jungle terrain.

URBAN GUERRILLA
   “Looks like we got some pigeons in the ’hood!”
   Unit Type: Infantry (conventional, battle armor)
   SPA Cost: 1 point
   The Urban Guerrilla ability is effective with
infantry squads that use any motive type, as long
as they can function within an urban environment
and enter buildings. Urban Guerrilla squad leaders
have trained their troops to use the ambient cover
of any urban or suburban setting, from buildings
and statues to parked vehicles and street lamps,
to maximum effect, enabling them to claim cover
even when out in the open. This ability applies a –1
roll modifier for all weapon attacks made against
members of an infantry squad led by an Urban
Guerrilla. It also reduces the damage from vehicular
weapons targeting infantry by eliminating the
double-damage effect for attacking infantry in
the open.
   Offensively, an Urban Guerrilla can call upon “local CSOXX
support” within an urban area once per scenario—
typically made up of armed residents ranging from                   The Axman BattleMech is perfectly suited for a Stone’s Revenants pilot with the Zweihander ability.
the neighborhood watch to local street thugs. When
called up, this “support” will take the form of a new                         ZWEIHANDER
Rifle (Ballistic) Foot Platoon with a Skill Rating of Green.                     “SMASH!”
   This “supporting infantry” will attack a target of the Urban                  Unit Type: ’Mechs
Guerrilla’s choice from any structure within 3 hexes of the Urban                SPA Cost: 2 points
Guerrilla’s position, but will scatter as soon as their numbers are              A MechWarrior with the Zweihander Ability has mastered the
reduced to half or less.                                                      ability to use his ’Mech’s muscles, melee weapons, and mass to
                                                                              their most devastating effect in close combat.

success exceeds the number of units available to move, do not carry      target for its Blood Stalker SPA.
over to later turns. For example, an Overrun-capable force makes
an Initiative roll of 11, while its opponent rolls a 6. That gives the   SHARP SHOOTERS
Overrun-capable force a margin of success of 5, allowing two units          A portion of the Force with this SCA specializes in long-range
to move and fire before any opposing units may move (5 divided           attacks at the expense of short-range accuracy. A third of the Force,
by 2 is 2.5, rounded down to 2).                                         rounded down, may be designated as Sharp Shooters and uses the
   Units employing Overrun Combat do not take part in the normal         following range modifiers in place of the standard range modifiers:
move/fire sequence and so do not count when determining how              Short +1, Medium +2, Long +3, Extreme +4. If used in conjunction
many units each side must move or fire when alternating turns            with another ability that alters their range modifiers, Sharp Shooters
during the Movement and Weapon Attack phases. Overrunning units          receive a +1 To-Hit modifier at Short range and a –1 modifier at
make physical attacks as normal during the Physical Attack Phase.        Long and Extreme range.

                         The Great Houses attempt to exploit planets for whatever resources they have even when scraping the edge of a star's outer life zone.


                                                                                                  In the following guidelines, references to “current knowledge,”
     SOLAR SYSTEM                                                                              “modern astronomers” or the like refer to circa 2016 astronomy
                                                                                               and astrophysics.
      GENERATION                                                                                  A blank star system and a blank planet construction sheet at the
                                                                                               end of the book (pp. 237-238) may be used as a template for filling
                                                                                               out important details.

                                                                                                  The defining feature of a star system is the star, also known
methods tend to produce star systems lacking habitable planets,
                                                                                               as the system primary. This star affects planetary formation and
so players should feel free to override the dice rolls if they want a

habitable planet or specific star system layout.
