                                                       ALL THE PRETTY COLORS                                                                         i


      The rules for generating the system primary are based                 Sol is a G2 star, meaning it verges on the whitish F-class, while
   on standard stellar classifications, as used by modern                   Tau Ceti is a G8 star, almost an orange-ish K-type star.
   astronomers. The basic stellar classification is a quick way of              BattleTech has mostly overlooked a third element of stellar                INTRODUCTION
   describing a star’s key characteristics: surface temperature             classification, a Roman numeral that describes the size of a
   and color (which is based on temperature), and size. From left           star (and, roughly, its stage in the stellar lifecycle). This value
   to right, a stellar classification (for example, G2V) consists of        is important because, for example, Betelgeuse and GJ 1046
                                                                                                                                                              FORCE
   a letter roughly describing the star’s temperature, a number             are both (approximately) M2 stars, but Betelgeuse is a super                     CREATION
   serving as a “decimal place” for the letter that more precisely          giant while GJ 1046 is a dwarf star. These Roman numerals,
   defines the temperature, and a Roman numeral defining the                those used in these rules, range from VI (sub-dwarf) to I (super
   star’s size. A full treatment of stellar classifications can be          giant stars). In between those values, V refers to the common
   found in astronomical reference books or on the Internet.                main sequence dwarfs (the longest phase of a star’s lifecycle)                    FORCE
                                                                                                                                                            OPERATIONS
      The stellar classes used in these guidelines are O, B, A, F,          and IV, III, and II refer to increasingly large giant stars that have
   G, K, and M. Specialty designations like D, L, T, and Y classes          left the main sequence of the stellar life-cycle. (Several other
   are a level of detail beyond the scope of these rules. In order          classes are used by astronomers, but are outside the scope of
   of temperature and their appearance on the Primary Stats                 these rules.) The system transit and recharge rules overlook                     FORMATION
   Table, M represents the “reddest,” coolest normal stars; Ks are          this aspect, and it is standard practice in current BattleTech                    BUILDING
   warmer orange-ish stars; Gs are yellow stars; Fs are yellow-             publications to change stellar types to main sequence. By
   white stars; As are white or bluish-white; Bs are blue; and              default, the transit and recharge rules refer to a class V main
   Os are deep blue (with most of their output actually in the              sequence star, and thus these guidelines refer only to class V                  INNER SPHERE
                                                                                                                                                               AT WAR
   ultraviolet range). The colors are related to temperature: cool          stars as well. Players should feel free to use non-main sequence
                                                                                                                                                            CONVERSIONS
   stars are a deep red. As stars become hotter, their color shifts         stars for their systems, but this will alter transit and recharge
   through orange, yellow, white, and eventually to blue shades.            times to values outside of the scope of existing transit and
                                                                                                                                                         INNER SPHERE
      After selecting the basic type, the other part of stellar             recharge rules. The effect of larger luminosity classes is                   SOLAR SYSTEM
                                                                                                                                                            AT WAR
   classification widely used in BattleTech (especially the JumpShip        discussed under Hot, Hot, Hot! (see p. 116).                                  GENERATION
                                                                                                                                                         CONVERSIONS
   recharging and system transit rules) is a suffix of 0-9 applied              Combined, these guidelines provide three-value stellar
   to O, B, A, F, G, K and M-type stars. This is a “decimal place” for      classifications. Betelgeuse’s full classification is M2Ia while
   the notation that helps gauge where a star is within its color           GJ 1046 is M2V, Sol is G2V and Tau Ceti is G8V.
                                                                                                                                                               LINKED
   classification. This is used because not all stars of a given type are       Players should also note that some variation still exists                    SCENARIOS
   created equal. For example, Tau Ceti (known as “New Earth” in            within specific classifications. For example, Sol and Alpha
   BattleTech) is a yellow, G-type star, but it is noticeably cooler and    Centauri-A (the brightest of the three Alpha Centauri stars)
   dimmer than Earth’s G-type star, Sol. To make these distinctions,        are both G2V, but Alpha Centauri A is somewhat more
   0 is set at the hot end of a spectral class while 9 is the cool end.     massive and brighter than Sol.                                                   NARRATIVE
                                                                                                                                                             CAMPAIGNS

real distribution of stars in the galaxy, which heavily favors

all stars, respectively, are too rare to be generated randomly               3        M             M              B            2            2               CREATION
from this column and so are left to players’ arbitrary decisions             4        G             M              A            3            4
or the use of the Hot Stars column.                                                                                                                         MAINTENANCE,

to favor terrestrial-type life and planets.                                                                                                                CUSTOMIZATION

for hosting a star system. Because class O stars are so rare and             9        M              F             A                                          RECORD

sequence) stars, as described in All the Pretty Colors (see above).

   As discussed in All the Pretty Colors (see p. 99), there are additional                    ORBITAL PLACEMENT TABLE
components to the stellar classification after the letter. To generate
the subtype from a selection of subtypes between 0 and 8, roll 1D6                                 Base Location                      Base Location
and refer to the Subtype column of the Primary Generation Table.                     Slot #            (AU)              Slot #           (AU)
Players should note that it is easy to substitute other types of dice                  1                0.4                9               38.8
to generate a full range of subtypes between 0 and 9, such as a D10
                                                                                       2                0.7               10               77.2
or D20. Also note that simple summations of dice (e.g., 2D4 or 2D6)
do not generate an even distribution of results. For example, 2D6                      3                1.0               11               154
is six times as likely to give a 7 as a 2.                                             4                1.6               12              307.6
                                                                                       5                2.8               13              614.8

Step 4: Planetary Details                                                       Ve = 11,186 m/s x (Diameter ÷ 12,742) x √ (Density ÷ 5.5153)
  Now determine the finer details of the objects in the system’s
orbital slots: number of moons, habitability, gravity, and so forth.             In this equation, Diameter is the diameter of the planet in
                                                                              kilometers and Density is the density of the planet in g/cm3. Again,
Common Details                                                                this is a ratio with respect to Terra’s diameter (12,742 kilometers),
  Several features, like gravity, apply to any object in a system. Other      density, and escape velocity (11,186 m/s). To convert escape
objects, like asteroid belts, have details that are handled separately.       velocity to hexes per turn, divide Ve by 300. To convert to kph,
  Surface Gravity: The gravity of a planet (or other spherical                multiply Ve by 3.6.
object) in Gs may be calculated with the following equation:                     The velocity of a circular, low-altitude orbit (within a few hundred
                                                                              kilometers of the surface) may be found by dividing Ve by the square
         Gravity = (Diameter ÷ 12742) x (Density ÷ 5.5153)                    root of 2 (1.414 as a quick approximation). While not related to Solar
                                                                              System Generation, it is a useful value to begin determining how
   Diameter is in kilometers (km) and Density is in grams per cubic           long a spacecraft lingers near a given point over an object (such as
centimeter (g/cm3). The equation is a simple ratio with Terra’s               to determine how many turns may be spent orbitally bombarding

  Asteroid belts are generated through several steps. First, a belt’s         • To calculate the density of a specific medium or small asteroid,
basic population multiplier is calculated compared to Sol’s asteroid            use the roll on the Asteroid Belt row of the Object Type Table.
belt. Second, the number of significant asteroids is calculated based           As belts often represent mixed leftovers from the birth of a star
on the belt’s density and its distance from the primary. These steps            system, the composition of an asteroid belt will not be uniform,
are repeated for each asteroid belt in the system. The belt’s basic             so density should be rolled for each individual asteroid.
population multiplier is calculated as follows:
                                                                            Moons and Rings
 Asteroid Population Multiplier = (Belt’s Orbit ÷ 2.8) x [1D6 ÷ 3]2            Larger objects tend to acquire natural satellites (moons) either
                                                                            during planetary creation or in subsequent events (asteroid capture
   In this equation, the belt’s orbit is the orbital distance from the      or massive grazing impacts). Gas giants are particularly noteworthy
primary in AU. Do not round at any step in the calculation.                 collectors of moons, with the known examples of Saturn and Jupiter
   This value of asteroid population multiplier is then used to             both possessing sixty or more moons at the writing of these rules.
multiply the following values to determine the number of these                 In reality, retention of moons depends on a number of factors
classes of asteroids in the belt (rounding normally):                       and can make large, far-flung collections of moons a difficult
                                                                            proposition due to various issues. Large neighboring planets are one
  • Dwarf terrestrials (500 kilometers+ diameter): 4                        such problem; for example, Jupiter periodically flips Mars on its ear,
  • Medium Asteroids (100-500 kilometers diameter): 200                     prevented the formation of a planet between Mars and Jupiter, and
  • Small Asteroids (1-100 kilometers diameter): 1,200,000                  prevents Saturn and Uranus from retaining many “Trojan” asteroids. A
                                                                            close gas giant will likewise make a widespread moon system difficult
        Since he wants a fairly advanced society that has nosed             to retain for its neighbors. A nearby primary can present a similar
     around the system and might have some off-planet bases,                problem for inner planets. Further, a small planet (like Earth) with a
     Chuck decides to work out the details of the asteroid belt in          large moon (like Luna) is unlikely to keep other moons for long (on
     Amtor-2’s system. He already knows that the belt is 0.6AU              time scales of mere centuries and millennia) because the large moon
     from the star. He rolls [1D6÷3]2 and gets a 5, then divides 5 by       ends up scattering other moons, usually driving them into collisions
     3 to get 1.667, and squares it to 2.778. The asteroid population       with the planet or the larger moon. Planets may also collect and lose
     multiplier is [0.6 AU ÷ 2.8 AU] x 2.778 = 0.595. Applying the          moons over their lives, as might be the case with the Martian moons
     multiplier, Chuck finds there are 2 dwarf terrestrials (4 x 0.595,     and many of the outer moons of Sol’s giant planets. Players should
     rounded), 119 medium asteroids (200 x 0.595, rounded), and             keep those factors in mind when using the random moon generation

a lot of orbital positions untenable for other moons. As another                        plagiarize those distributions. (The gas giants have yet to retain                  RECORD

example, an orbit too close to a planet may result in the moon                          counsel to defend their intellectual property.)
breaking up (“Roche’s Limit”). In the absence of a succinct version                       As another alternative, players can simply skip this step. The
of satellite distribution, the following guidelines are suggested:                      exact distribution of moons around a planet in BattleTech rarely

   The atmosphere of a planet depends to some extent on its size             atmosphere composition is detailed below.
class, which generally indicates how much atmosphere it might                   In this step of planet creation, even more than the others, players
retain. Dwarf terrestrials, for example, are too small to retain             should remember these rolls and tables are merely guidelines. If a
atmospheres, while terrestrial planets are usually unable to retain          GM needs a new habitable planet for a campaign, they shouldn’t
hydrogen/helium atmospheres.                                                 keep rolling up new star systems until they get a habitable planet.
   Dwarf Terrestrials: Dwarf terrestrials will not have atmospheres in       Instead, make a planet in the life zone habitable, and forget the dice.
the conventional sense. To be scientifically precise, they might have        These guidelines are to help players create a random star system
some individual gas molecules flitting around them (as is the case           that is typical of the BattleTech universe (in which most star systems
with Mercury’s and Luna’s “exospheres”), but this would constitute           are uninhabitable).
a vacuum in BattleTech. Some ultra-cold, icy dwarf terrestrials like            It should also be noted that the incidence of habitable planets
Pluto may retain a slightly more detectable atmosphere, but this is          using this chart is far above the norm for BattleTech, which is about
also a vacuum under BattleTech rules. Moons of this size may retain          1 habitable planet per 1,000 systems in the Inner Sphere—let alone
atmospheres if they orbit a gas giant (see Options, p. 115).                 reality. This is done for playability. For the same reason, players
   Gas and Ice Giants: Ice giants and gas giants have lots of hydrogen       should also feel free to declare a planet uninhabitable rather than
and helium, plus traces of water, methane, etc. Giants closer to the         depending on dice rolls to create vast numbers of habitable worlds
star than the life zone may exhibit extremely hot atmospheres with           in their explorations.
unusual trace additions, like silicate and metal gases. In any case,            For terrestrial planet atmospheres, two rolls are made using the
these are very high and toxic atmospheres (and no solid surface              Atmospheric Pressure and Habitability Table. One roll, Atmospheric
to stand on, at least no surface that human technology can reach).           Pressure, is for all terrestrial planets in the star system. The second
   Giant Terrestrials: These large planets are likely to have a              roll, Habitability, is only for terrestrial planets in the system’s life
considerable atmosphere by the standards of human-habitable                  zones. (Atmospheres outside the life zone are automatically treated
planets, but they are atmospheric (and “ice mantle”) lightweights            as Toxic.)
by the standards of ice and gas giants. To determine giant terrestrial          The Atmospheric Pressure Roll is 2D6, modified by two factors.
atmospheric composition, roll 1D6. On a result of 1-5, the giant             First, if the planet is closer to the star than the life zone, subtract 2
terrestrial has an atmosphere like a gas giant of hydrogen, helium,          from the roll to reflect the fact hot planets tend to lose atmospheres.
and trace gases (see Gas Giant above), though the atmosphere is              Second, because escape velocity determines how well a planet
“only” 1-5 percent of the planet’s mass (for comparison, Venus’s super-      retains an atmosphere, divide the escape velocity of the planet (in
dense pre-terraforming atmosphere was 0.01 percent of Venus’s                m/s) by 11,186 m/s (without rounding), multiply the result by the
mass). On a result of 6, the planet has an atmosphere like a terrestrial     (modified) die roll, and then round the final answer to the nearest
planet, and thus uses the terrestrial planet section on atmospheres.         integer normally. Consult the Atmospheric Pressure Column of
                                                                             the table to determine the planet’s atmosphere. The resulting

                7                              Nitrogen                       Carbon Dioxide                   Methane                  Hydrochloric Acid   †

**Roll again once on this column (ignoring 11 or 12) and once on the Special Traces Table
†
 Chemical makes the atmosphere Caustic (see p. 54, TO:AR)


                                                                                                                                                                            109
                                                                        Players may also be tempted to create worlds with exotic
                                                                     atmospheres like pure chlorine, xenon, or sulfur hexafluoride,
                                                                     but should keep several things in mind. First, the details of
                                                                     nucleosynthesis in stars make lighter elements more common than
                                                                     any element with more protons (a higher atomic number) than iron.
                                                                     These details also make elements with odd atomic numbers, with
                                                                     the exception of hydrogen, rarer than those with an even number.
                                                                     Thus, halogens (fluorine, chlorine, etc.) tend to be rarer than elements
                                                                     like oxygen and sulfur. The rarity of halogens is also increased by
                                                                     their chemical aggressiveness, which makes them more likely to be
                                                                     bound in minerals. Other chemical behaviors of the elements and
                                                                     planetary evolution tend to limit the range of gases in atmospheres.
                                                                     Oxygen, for example, prefers to form water, carbon dioxide and rocks
                                                                     over existing as a free gas. In the absence of other observations,
                                                                     the atmospheric gases found in the Sol system can be taken as
                                                                     representative of gases elsewhere—the differences will be relative
                                                                     quantity (percentages), not types of gas. An interesting and realistic
                                                                     example of the range of terrestrial planet atmospheres can be found
                                                                     by researching the evolution of Earth’s atmosphere from its primary,
                                                                     to secondary, and finally modern compositions.

                                                                     Planetary Temperatures
                                                                        While the calculations for planetary surface temperatures are
                                                                     relatively straightforward for airless planets (assuming the player knows
                                                                     the planet’s albedo and solar illumination), planets with atmospheres
                                                                     become quite complicated. Exact temperatures are rarely important
   While Chuck will handle Amtor-2’s atmosphere (that of             for most planets, and therefore the following guidelines apply:

component, and the trace component. He gets 9, 10, and                     and there is no surface for BattleMechs to operate on (and so
12, respectively, which are modified to 6 and 7 while the 12               no need to worry about the multi-thousand degree heat of
remains unchanged.                                                         what passes for the planet’s surface).

   If a planet is habitable, it will almost certainly have life unless    • Disease/Virus: A native or imported microbe or virus has
some natural disaster has occurred or humans recently destroyed             found humans to be fertile ground despite humanity’s
the ecosystem. (Note that mature ecosystem such as Terra’s                  advanced medicine. This pesky disease may be fatal or just
extends some kilometers into the crust and ocean floor—it takes             debilitating; examples of both can be found in prior BattleTech
some effort to completely destroy an ecosystem). This is because            publications (the Brisbane Virus, Cusset Crud, Fenris Flu, Laen’s
atmospheres with enough oxygen to support human life do not                 Regret, Eltanin Brain Fever, Black Marsh Fever, Waimalu Fever,
last without artificial or biological intervention—oxygen is a              Skokie Shivers, etc.). This is primarily a roleplaying concern that
chemically aggressive element that will quickly bind itself into            will not manifest in a BattleTech board game.
rocks, depleting the world’s oxygen somewhere between millennia           • Incompatible Biochemistry: Quite frequently, humans have
and a few million years. Therefore, a habitable planet will have            found that native life forms are not edible and the ecosystem
either native or imported (terraformed) life able to support the            may even resist human crops and animal imports. The problem
habitable environment.                                                      rarely lasts long if the planet is targeted for colonization—

Earth (5.51 g/cm3), Mercury (5.43 g/cm3) and Venus (5.2 g/cm3)          implausible” means “quite fun,” go for it.

MATH GUIDANCE                                                             annoying to others. Since D6 rolls fall outside the normal order of
   A number of calculations in the Solar System Generation rules          mathematical operations, use the following procedure: first, make
involve more complicated math than usually found in BattleTech.           the specified D6 roll (typically 1D6). Second, apply the exponent to
However, the most intimidating calculations consist only of unusual       the D6 result. Third, add the specified value (if any).
exponents and long, but simple equations. If players remember                For asteroid belts, the roll is: 1D61.15. As an example, if the D6 roll
mathematical parentheses and the order of operations (or take             is 2, then the result is 2^1.15 = 2.219. If the D6 roll is a 6, then the
a moment to look up mathematical order of operations on the               density is 6^1.15 = 7.850.
Internet), they should encounter few problems.                               For terrestrial planets, the roll is 2.5 + 1D60.75. First roll D6. As an
   The Object Type Table is a good example, as it includes raising        example, assume the result is a 3. Apply the exponent: 3^0.75 =
numbers to 1.15 and 0.75. Either exponent can be applied with             2.2795. Then add 2.5: 4.7795.
a typical scientific calculator or the calculator accessory program
commonly found on personal computers. The function is usually             Surface Gravity Calculations
displayed on common scientific calculators as “^”, “x^y,” or “yx.”           This equation is relatively straightforward if the order of
   In fact, the other significant math in this chapter is square roots,   operations is observed. Applying the divisors in the wrong place
which are a special case of exponents: applying a square root to          is the most likely cause of a problem:
a number is the same as raising the number to the 0.5th power.
   Example problems for the larger equations used in these rules                   Gravity = (Diameter ÷ 12742) x (Density ÷ 5.5153)
are provided below.

                                                                         Population
                   COLONY                                                   The Planetary Population Table below captures the general diffusion
                                                                         and growth of humanity beyond the confines of Terra. Until the fall
                  CREATION                                               of the Star League, the “borders” of the Inner Sphere were porous.
                                                                         Coupled with habitable planets in the Periphery being no less common
   These guidelines are intended to help players describe populated      than in the Inner Sphere, this led to humanity continuing to spread
planets such as those found in the BattleTech universe. The process      away from Terra in ever-thinning density. However, the Succession
involves several steps, beginning with determination of the              Wars sharply curtailed the number of JumpShips available to the bulk
population, which will influence a number of subsequent steps.           of humankind, either by destroying the ships outright or destroying
   The second step provides guidelines to determine a planet’s           their shipyards, which the Star League had concentrated in the Inner
Universal Socio-Industrial Level Rating, or USILR. The USILR code        Sphere. The Clans, on the other hand, have a very restricted population
uses an A through F grading system to represent (in order): a            to work with, which limits the size of their colonies.
settled world’s level of technological sophistication; industrial           The first row of the Planetary Population Table addresses a
development; dependence on imported raw materials; industrial            special circumstance: an Inner Sphere world depopulated by
output; and agricultural dependence (both of the latter relative         the Succession Wars and subsequently removed from the maps
to population).                                                          by ComStar, who judged it uninhabited. More than 200 planets
   The third step is to determine the planet’s style of government. In   experienced that fate after they were directly attacked by weapons
cases where the planet is a member of an interstellar, multi-system      of mass destruction or suffered from collapsing technology, and
faction, the planetary government is sometimes different than the        there are a rare few failed colonies from before the Succession Wars
interstellar government (many of which function as alliances or          that were likewise marked off the maps. However, humans are hard
confederacies and thus are tolerant of differences in member-            to completely exterminate and some of those worlds may still host
                                                                         small populations. Other worlds in the Inner Sphere would use the
planet governance).
                                                                         second row of the Planetary Population Table.
   Finally, a number of secondary planetary characteristics such
                                                                            To determine a colony’s population, the Planetary Population
as the presence of recharge stations and HPGs are determined.
                                                                         Table first requires players to select, either from the results of Step
                                                                         1A or by their own choice, who settled the colony, when, and
STEP 1: POPULATION                                                       how far the colony is from Terra in light-years. Once the founding
  Determining a planet’s population is a straightforward set of          culture, era and distance is known, the player cross-references the
three rolls. The results determine when the colony was settled and       Distance row with the Founding Era column to find what dice rolls
by whom, and the population itself.                                      are required to determine the population.
                                                                            The Planetary Population Table has two numbers for a given
Step 1A: History of Occupancy                                            combination of distance, era, and founding culture. The number to
  If the player does not have a preference for who settled the           the left of the colon is the result of a 1D6 roll that determines whether
colony (non-Clan or Clan) and when it was settled (Star League           the colony falls into a low or high population category; the number to
era, earlier, or more recently), a 1D6 roll on the Occupancy History     the right of the colon is the second roll that specifies the population.
Table will determine those aspects. Alternatively, players may use       For example, a planet inside the Inner Sphere settled during the Star
a date and settling faction of their own choosing. The date and          League lists 1-5: 50 million x 4D6, and 6: 500 million x 4D6. This means
settlement are then used in Step 1B.                                     a 1D6 roll of 1-5 requires a second roll of 4D6 multiplied by 50 million
                                                                         to determine the population, while a 1D6 result of 6 requires a second
                                                                         roll of 4D6 multiplied by 500 million. (This reflects the tendency of
                                                                         most BattleTech planets to be modestly populated, while some thrive.

care equivalent to twentieth-century level (at best).

   Planets with clear governments based on published data do                 Autocracy/Oligarchy: These are highly authoritarian
not roll on the Base Government Table. The Clan homeworlds,                governments with little input from the common citizen/subject.
for example, automatically fall under Clan-style government (a             Autocracies are ruled by one person (an absolute monarchy,
caste-driven, warrior-dominant hierarchy). Planets in the Capellan         a hereditary dictatorship), while oligarchies are ruled by small
Confederation and Draconis Combine are automatically oligarchies           groups (a military junta, a religious council, a corporate board,
or autocracies. Planets in most other existing multi-world factions        feudal lords).
tend to allow some diversity in their planetary governments.                 Clan: Clan governments are an unusual form of oligarchy.
   Players determine a planet’s government by rolling 2D6 on               Overall Clan government consists of five castes (warriors, scientists,
the Base Government Table. The roll may be modified using the              merchants, technicians, laborers), each with their own ruling
modifiers listed in the Base Government Table, depending on the            councils and ranks. The civilian castes handle most minutia of
faction to which the planet belongs, though that may require a             running Clan society, with the warrior caste rubber-stamping
judgment call based on limited data. For example, determining              most civilian decisions except the overall direction of the Clan. Clan
whether ancient factions like the Chesterton Trade League were             government also comes with a peculiar Communist-style economic
liberal or authoritarian is difficult, and in such situations the player   system where the state controls all means of production, and
should use a modifier of 0.                                                economic activities outside the bare necessities are suppressed.
   The Base Government Table provides a handful of broad                   Most invading Clans learned to rule their conquests with a light
government classes (base governments, sometimes with                       hand, leaving planetary governments and economies untouched
preliminary qualifiers) listed roughly in order of the level of control    due to significant disparity in populations, thus forming de facto
and freedom. After generating a result from the roll on the Base           confederacies with a Clan central government and differing
Government Table, players may apply additional qualifiers. This is         planetary governments.
done by looking at the base government’s row on the Detailed                 Democracy: These governments grant electoral or even
Government Table and selecting modifiers (if any) that suit the            legislative authority in varying levels to the citizenry. The
player’s concept of the colony’s government.                               variations are vast: federal republics, confederations, constitutional
   Definitions of the base government types and government                 monarchies, and many more.
qualifiers follow the Base and Detailed Government Tables.                   Dictatorship: These highly authoritarian governments are ruled
                                                                           by a single individual. Long-lived dictatorships tend to evolve into
Government Definitions                                                     autocracies or oligarchies.
  The following definitions are supplied in an absolute form.
That is, they are stripped of political stylings of their members.            Government Modifiers: The following terms further refine the
They describe a planet’s government as it actually exists, not as          type of government.
the government describes itself. (Many dictatorial governments                Athenian/Cyberdemocracy: An Athenian democracy directly
have described themselves as liberal democracies, but these                engages every member of the electorate in legislative and
rules would call such governments dictatorships. Similarly, the            sometimes executive decisions. The electorate may be extremely
name of a major faction, like the Outworlds Alliance or Lyran              small (for a historical example, this may be “all free male wealthy
Commonwealth, says little about the actual form of government.)            landowners”), but each member of it participates in legislative
                                                                           decisions. A cyberdemocracy is a minor variation on an Athenian
   Base Governments: The definitions below form the basis for              democracy where polling is handled electronically. This modifier
most governments in human space. These base types may be                   only applies to Democratic and Oligarchic governments.
complemented by the modifiers listed in the Government Table.                 Communist: An ideal Communist society would be classless,
   Anarchy: Not so much a government as a lack of one, these               moneyless, and stateless. In practice (and for these rules),
“governments” rarely cover more area than a province, though the           Communism refers to governments where almost all businesses
post-Civil War Terran Hegemony was an interstellar anarchy until           are owned by the state. It is not a comment on how authoritarian
conquered by the Houses. Anarchies rarely last long, generally             the government might be; after the twentieth century, humanity
only surviving while potential leaders or power groups have no             did manage to create a few liberal Communist governments on its
advantage over each other.                                                 many far-flung worlds.
   At the player’s discretion, anarchies may also represent a                 Confederacy/Alliance: Confederacies and commonwealths are
situation where the planet has multiple governments (each rolled           unions of smaller provincial governments that grant substantial
up and detailed separately per the steps above), but no overarching        powers to those member-governments. Alliances are even looser
planetary government. If the player does not have a preference,            associations of governments. This contrasts with federations,
roll 1D6. If a 1 or 2 results, then roll 1D6 again to determine the        which tend to grant greater authority to the central government.
number of governments. If this result is 3-5, then roll 2D6 x 10 to        Confederacies and alliances may be applied to almost any type
determine the number of governments; if the result is a 6, roll 3D6        of government.
x 100 to determine the number of governments.
   Constitutional: A constitutional government imposes a            into governance. Civil rights are restricted and elections may
fundamental set of laws around which the government is              be frequently rigged. In some cases, this stems from corruption.
organized. This is a staple of most governments and thus            In others, it derives from a very limited electorate (for example,
does not need to be stated, but some governments—                   only nobles or major landowners or corporate shareholders may
like autocracies, dictatorships, and monarchies—are not             participate). Despite their limitations, these governments have
                                                                                                                                           INTRODUCTION
necessarily run to constitutional standards, and thus               broader participation and more civil rights than autocracies
it is worth noting when such governments adhere to                  and oligarchies.
a constitution.                                                        Monarchy: These governments consolidate their sovereignty
   Corporate: This type of government is overtly run                into one person. The difference between an autocracy (absolute            FORCE
by corporations, with the government contracting                    monarchy) and representative democracy (constitutional                   CREATION
out most services to corporate entities. This definition            monarchy, parliamentary democracy) depends on the level of
specifically applies to governments where corporations              authority granted to the monarch. Long-lived monarchies tend
supply most services, not to other types of governments             to be less authoritative (if not necessarily less powerful) than
                                                                                                                                              FORCE
that are simply heavily influenced or informally ruled by           younger monarchies because, as an ancient monarch once said,
                                                                                                                                            OPERATIONS
corporate/business interests (which are better classified           “The buck stops here.” In other words, the more a monarch is
as oligarchies or autocracies). Corporate qualifiers                associated with unpopular legislation and executive decisions,
apply to almost any type of government, though                      the shorter their term in office (and the greater the frequency
most commonly democracies.                                          of regicide).                                                            FORMATION
   Delegated Democracy: A hybrid of Athenian and                       Parliamentary: Parliamentary governments include                       BUILDING
representative democracies, this sort of government uses            some executive powers (head of government) in their
representatives for the electorates (like a representative          legislative bodies, compared to governments that assign
                                                                                                                                            INNER SPHERE
democracy) but ties the representatives’ votes rigidly to the       both head of state (e.g., monarch, premier, president)

include the Havatoo (a technocratic oligarchy), Kormor                                                       The Vepajans are ruled by a monarch held in near-divine

groups on Burroughs’ Amtor do not translate into fixed nations                                               long-lived government dominated by one person), with
(the displaced Vepajans, the Cloud People, and pirates), so                                                  the Monarchy qualifier. Chuck notes the Vepajans as a

players. So how does this translate into the rules?                                                              The Thorists were modeled on Communists as seen

as an anarchy (4 regional governments) on the planet                                                         some reference material on the Soviet Union for the time
construction sheet.                                                                                          and decides that a base government of oligarchy (a small

eugenicist super-scientists. The technocracy aspect has no                                                   is appropriate, as is the Communist qualifier, so he notes the
bearing (there are many forms of oligarchies: by engineers                                                   Thorists as a Communist oligarchy.
in the case of a technocracy, by Communist party leaders, by                                                     The Zani were modeled on mid-twentieth century fascist
colonial first families, by a feudal court, etc.). The key feature                                           governments near the outbreak of World War II. The Zanis
of such a government, Chuck sees, is that the Havatoo are                                                    were ruled by a single dictator and were violently racist.
ruled by a small clique: this means the base government is                                                   The base government is simple: dictatorship. Chuck sees

                                                                                                                                      STEP 4: OTHER FEATURES
                                                                                                                                         Finally, players may want to add other
                                                                                                                                      features to their colony worlds such
                                                                                                                                      as hyperpulse generators (HPGs) and
                                                                                                                                      recharge stations.
                                                                                                                                         Hyperpulse Generators: Introduced
                                                                                                                                      in 2630, the HPG substantially cut
                                                                                                                                      communication times previously
                                                                                                                                      bottlenecked by JumpShip transit. The
                                                                                                                                      two types of HPGs available for star
                                                                                                                                      systems are A-rated and B-rated. Both
                                                                                                                                      classes of HPGs have a 50-light-year
                                                                                                                                      range. Serving as communication hubs for
                                                                                                                                      B-rated HPGs, an A-rated HPG transmits
                                                                                                                                      to each B-rated HPG in range sequentially
                                                                                                                                      every 12 to 24 hours (sooner for priority
                                                                                                                                      messages and during the Star League).
                                                                                                                                      B-rated HPGs transmit less frequently
                                                                                                                                      than A-rated HPGs (every 12 hours during
                                                                                                                                      the Star League, or every few days after
                                                                                                                                      the fall of the Star League).
                                                                                                                                         ComStar also sometimes offers C- and
                                                                                                                                      D-rated services for Periphery planets
                                                                                                                                      near the Inner Sphere (within 550 light-
                                                                                                                                      years of Terra), which are not HPGs but
                                                                                                                                      rather courier JumpShips that provided
                                                                                                                                      scheduled message deliveries (visits every
                                                                                                                                      3 months in the case of C-rated service, or
                                                                                                                                      annually for D-rated service), plus offering
                                                                                                                                      subsidized collars to traders’ DropShips.
   Historically a nexus of manufacturing, the orbital space over the Lyran world of Alarion once bustled with interstellar traffic.

support facilities.                                                   being destroyed or retreating from the map, he wins a Marginal

                                                                                                                                                 RECORD
10 1-hex Medium standard buildings (CF 40) on the map,                   If 1-3 buildings are destroyed before the attacker is destroyed
                                                                                                                                                 SHEETS
each containing 10 percent of his stock of supplies (parts,           or retreats from the map, the defender wins a Marginal Victory.
ammunition and so on), and a single 1-hex Hardened standard              If no buildings are destroyed before the attacker is destroyed
building (CF 120) representing the defender’s command post.           or retreats from the map, the defender wins a Decisive Victory.
Control of these structures determines victory in the game.
These structures may be destroyed normally or may be captured
by having infantry Forces of one side take sole possession of

   A civilian population has been incited to riot, and an urban or industrial                 The capture of a small seaport area offers some unusual opportunities. Water will
area is being torn apart as a result. The rioters may or may not (at the GM’s             play a big role, which can make things difficult for some unit types, and easier for others
discretion) have been coerced or even actively assisted by an opposing irregular          (hovercraft, for example, are in heaven). Watercraft, rarely seen in many BattleTech
Force (such as Death Commandos or ROM operatives). The players will be tasked             games, become a handy thing as well. This includes blue-water naval support-size
with assisting law enforcement in getting things under control, and inflicting            units if the GM desires. The scope of such units can range from cargo-type vessels like
minimal casualties if possible. Alternatively, the players could have been hired          freighters or tankers to massive oceangoing warships. Perhaps one objective is to capture
by the irregulars to provide added muscle to the rioters in their attempts to push        the port intact. If a blue-water naval vessel is firing at the players with artillery weapons,
back law enforcement.                                                                     they’ll be forced to find a way to kill the ship before they can complete their objective.

need to capture it from the enemy guarding it—and inflict as little damage as             their opponents’ airpower. Wrinkles to this scenario could include aircraft taking
possible. What use is a captured ’Mech depot if it’s razed in the process? The GM         off to attack the players if they don’t move quickly enough, turreted defenses,

reinforcements to beat back, or even set a trap.

    Conversely, the players must prevent the enemy from capturing a strategic             important thing is that it needs to be destroyed. The mission is straightforward,
point. This can take place as an expanded portion of the latter part of a capture,        and the GM needs to carefully plan it out, lest a single Thumper shell or LRM salvo

REPAIR COST AND TIME                                                               repairable, particularly in instances where the vehicle’s crew were
   Not everyone enjoys doing excessive paperwork between                           killed, or motive hits forced its abandonment. The GM will have to use
missions, and keeping things simple can help a GM better enjoy                     their discretion: would this unit be able to be repaired realistically in
the fruits of their labors. The following table can be a handy way                 a brief amount of time between missions? As with ‘Mechs, determine
to let the players determine repairs based on the costs. An even                   what seems reasonable based on the damage the unit suffered. If
easier method can be to simply have the GM declare how long it                     the vehicle only needs some armor patched up, and maybe a thrown
will take for the players’ units to be repaired. While this can cause              tread or flat tire fixed, it might be available immediately. Further
some strife for players, as long as the GM is consistent and not                   damage might require more time. As one can imagine, any unit
cruel, it is manageable.                                                           destroyed by an ammunition explosion or engine destruction should
   A ’Mech that survives a mission and sustains only armor damage,                 be considered unsalvageable no matter the situation.
with no internal components destroyed, would likely be available
for the next mission. Modest internal damage, such as small                        RESULTS AND FOLLOW UP
weapons being damaged, actuators, or an engine hit might cause                        Once salvage has been claimed and repair times have been
a unit to miss a mission. Bigger problems, such as the destruction                 determined, the GM must apply the results of the mission to their
of a head or limb, damage to a major system such as multiple hits                  webbed campaign plans, and note any effects. Once these effects
to its engine or gyro, a ruined larger weapon like an autocannon,                  are resolved, the GM presents the next set of missions for the
etc., could cause a ’Mech to miss two or even three missions. This                 players to choose from.

   When it comes to winning a campaign, in some cases each              Building a starting Force for a campaign takes more planning
objective on a map will be valued equally, with victory simply      than for a single battle. For most campaigns, a good rule of thumb
a matter of capturing them all. In other campaigns, different       is to first decide the size of the Force that will be used in a typical
                                                                                                                                               INTRODUCTION
key locations will have different values, sometimes even            battle (lance, company, battalion). The size of a campaign Force
different values depending on who controls the objective.           will typically be one Force size larger. This method works best for
When determining the value of each objective on the map,            player Forces, as it allows the player to lose some Formations in a
the overall objective of the campaign must be kept in mind.         battle without the casualties drastically affecting the campaign.             FORCE

equal value. In a campaign of planetary conquest, capturing         a bit trickier. In-depth guidance can be found in Force Creation
the capital or controlling all the DropPorts would likely have      (see p. 8). A quick and easy method is to take the Force size for
more value than holding a few small cities.                         a typical battle (lance, company, etc.) and create one for each

simply start at one map edge like a typical battle.                          Battles take place within the framework of a Strategic Turn, which
   Defending forces can begin wherever the defender desires. Some         encompasses one of a number of actions (including combat) within
locations could be more lightly defended than others, in favor of         a discrete period of time. This is usually a day, but if players agree,
tough defenses at key locations. In other situations, a defender’s        the period of time may be increased (for example, to a week) or
forces would be equally divided to defend each objective. Each            shortened (perhaps to 6 hours) to reflect different tempos of combat
option has different merits and risks, and depends on the value of        and different scales of campaign play. The main impact of changing
each map objective and the overall campaign objective. The defender       this timing is for players and GMs that wish to specify the amount of
can even deploy part of all of their Force as Hidden Units (see p. 256,   time available when incorporating time-related mechanics, such as
TW), writing down their deployment locations on a piece of paper.         maintenance, repairs, fatigue, and so on (see Maintenance, Repairs,
                                                                          Customization and Salvage, p. 188). Within each Strategic Turn, the

     a regiment of 4 battalions, each with a command lance, and              Combat orders come in three forms: Attack, Scout, and Defend.

     DropShips before encountering the defenders.                            Scout: The Formation attempts to determine the size, composition,

       Brian has his DropShips land in Zones next to each other              Defend: When given a Defend order, a Formation is prepared for

     around the cities and supply depots.

    SCENARIO TYPE TABLE                                                   SCENARIO TYPE ROLL MODIFIERS
       1D6          Battle         Scout          Modifier      Orders
        1               EX          BK                          One Formation is given an Attack order and the other is given a non-combat

        2               EX          BK                          order (Repair, Move, or Fast Move)
        3               HTL         PB            +1 Battle     Both Formations are given Attack orders
        4               HTL         PB            Modifier      Orders
        5               SU          HS                          One Formation is given a Scout order and the other is given a non-combat
                                                  +3 Scout
        6               SU          HS                          order (Repair, Move, or Fast Move)
        7               HS          RR            +1 Scout      Both Formations are given Scout orders
        8               BK          RR            –3 Scout      One Formation is given Attack orders
        9               BK          RR
  BK = Breakthrough       EX = Extraction
  HS = Hide and Seek      HTL = Hold the Line
  PB = Probe              RR = Recon Raid
  SU = Stand Up Fight

a target, such as a secret weapon, an important diplomat or a           trapped behind enemy lines. To reach safety, the units must cross
computer memory core, from behind the defender’s lines.                 the map and break through the Defender’s Force.

edge and cannot be within four hexes of any other map edge. The         possess fewer than the needed number of mapsheets, simply use
Attacker then writes down the target hex’s number and mapsheet          all available mapsheets. Place the mapsheets in a single, long row
name on a slip of paper, which he folds and gives to a neutral          with their short edges touching.
party for safekeeping (or the Defender can simply put the paper            The Attacker then chooses one of the narrow edges of the joined
in their pocket without looking at it).                                 map as the place from which their Force will enter. The Attacker’s

                                                            MAPSHEET TABLES
        Unless otherwise described in a track, it is suggested to use 1 mapsheet for every 4 units used in the track, though this can
      be adjusted to better suit the track’s needs or to better fit with whatever size playing field is normal for the players involved.
      Some tracks may need bigger maps so that players can go around objectives or opposing units for a better approach, while
      some tracks would be better served with the opposing Force within LRM range of the players. Careful consideration of the
      number of maps used could help make the game enhanced or different from previous games.

                                                                                                                               listed in a given track is the maximum

                                                                                                                               to take fewer units at their own discretion,
                                                                                                                               however.
                                                                                                                                  Opponents should always calculate the          NARRATIVE
                                                                                                                               size of opposing Forces using the player’s        CAMPAIGNS
                                                                                                                               deployed Force, never the total Force the

     percent of the Defender (player’s deployed Force), and so           It represents opportunities, whether to participate in a particular

advanced technologies units to find the Force’s overall Technology

fashion to the TR formula. To find a Force’s Equipment Rating, simply                            activities during this period between tracks. Any unused SPs are set

     ’Mechs, six Standard Rules Inner Sphere ’Mechs and two                                         Players can use SP to make repairs and to purchase new units or

        The percentages of these units in the Force are computed                                 unit Cost Table. For personnel, use SP Activity Personnel Cost Table.
     as follows:                                                                                 All new personnel are considered Green unless the player spends
        Inner Sphere Standard Rules units = (6 / 12) x 100 = 50                                  double or triple the cost as indicated in the table footnote.
     percent                                                                                        While a player generally has access to any RAT, for better

     Standard Rules Inner Sphere/Clan technology is thus 83.33                                   their own custom RAT that addresses their specific campaign needs.
     percent. As this is less than 85 percent, the unit therefore has                               Localized RAT: Use a RAT that is specific to the region of space
     a B Technology Rating Modifier.                                                             where the last track occurred. [Example: A player purchasing new
                                                                                                                            units after recently completing a track on
                                                                                                                            Northwind in the Jihad era would select
                                                                                                                            a unit off of the Word of Blake RAT, as the
                                                                                                                            track takes place in the time period of the
                                                                                                                            Word of Blake Protectorate.]
                                                                                                                               Origin RAT: The player uses a RAT that
                                                                                                                            is specific to the region of space from
                                                                                                                            where the unit originates. [Example: A
                                                                                                                            player using the First FedSuns Lancers
                                                                                                                            would use a House Davion/Federated
                                                                                                                            Suns RAT.]
                                                                                                                               For-hire RAT: The player uses only
                                                                                                                            Mercenary, Militia, or Periphery RATs
                                                                                                                            to purchase units at the listed SP cost;
                                                                                                                            selecting any other RAT would be double
                                                                                                                            (or triple) the SP cost.
                                                                                                                               SP can also be converted to C-bills
                                                                                                                            to allow for the purchase of other
                                                                                                                            components not expressly covered
                                                                                                                            by these rules, such as DropShips,
                                                                                                                            JumpShips, support vehicles, and their
                                                                                                                            attendant crews.

   Between tracks, personnel that participated in the last track may

listed Support Point cost. This represents that while personnel can

                                                                                              objectives achieved, salvage taken, and so on.

by one, or improve both his Gunnery and Piloting Rating by one,

  BUILDING YOUR                                                             If building a Mission-style track, there are a few changes

  OWN CAMPAIGN
                                                                         to the process that the GM should keep in mind to
                                                                         streamline these more basic tracks. To start, eliminate the        INTRODUCTION
                                                                         Situation and Aftermath sections.
   The following rules are meant as a guide, and not a hard-and-            Options should be restricted to one. In some cases, if the
fast ruleset for campaign construction. As always, players should        GM feels it is needed due to the needs of the group or the
                                                                                                                                               FORCE
use whatever fits their particular group’s style of gameplay and         plot, one additional Option can be added.                            CREATION
should feel free to adjust other material as needed.                        Keep Objectives simple, and often to no more than two.
   The best way to build your own Custom Chaos campaign                     There should ideally be no more than one Special
is to first construct your campaign arc using the Map-Based              Rule in play, not counting any “blanket” rules set for the
Campaign or the Narrative Campaign rules presented elsewhere             entire campaign.                                                      FORCE
in this book. This gives you a starting framework, which can                                                                                 OPERATIONS
                                                                            Lastly, the Next Track section should provide only one
then be used to build your core track progression and work               or two track directions, often to get the players back into
out additional pathways, if needed.                                      the flow of the main plot of the campaign.
   The guiding principle of Custom Chaos is to provide players with                                                                          FORMATION
a series of games that have a “choose your own adventure” feel to                                                                             BUILDING
them, giving options at various points both inside and outside of      GAME SETUP
the track. This customizes the experience for each player group,          The next section is one of the most important, as this
and can also help allot for available time, Skill Rating, and other                                                                         INNER SPHERE
                                                                       is where both sides of the conflict are defined and the                 AT WAR
factors that often impact overall gameplay. Because this principle     battlefield constructed.                                             CONVERSIONS
emphasizes “choice,” it is important that players are presented with      Unless the GM has a particular battlefield setup in mind,
a minimum of two choices for various track sections.                   it is suggested that the players be allowed to randomly             INNER SPHERE
   If desired, a GM can add generic mission tracks such as those       determine the playing area. That area can be tailored to a          SOLAR SYSTEM
                                                                                                                                              AT WAR
found in Total Chaos (pp. 30-38) or the Starterbook series as part     specific terrain table, such as woods, mountains, or desert.         GENERATION
                                                                                                                                           CONVERSIONS
of the campaign. These provide a twofold benefit: providing small      Terrain tables provide map selections from the older BattleTech
skirmishes that can provide extra salvage and WP opportunities,        Map Sets; if these products are not available, the tables can
and offering more varied detail for the player campaign.               instead provide inspiration as to how the battlefield may be            LINKED
   A Custom Chaos track typically contains the following               constructed using standard terrain pieces or through custom           SCENARIOS
sections: Situation, Game Setup, Warchest, Options, Objectives,        map making software.
Special Rules, Aftermath, and Next Track. Some track variants             Next, set up the Attacker and Defender selections. If the
also include Victory Conditions.                                       group is running a Force through the campaign, assign them
   However, when building a track, the GM should proceed in                                                                                  NARRATIVE
                                                                       to the side that works within the context of the mission. For         CAMPAIGNS
the following order: Game Setup, Objectives, Options, Warchest,        some examples, see the Attacker/Defender Assignment Tablet.
Special Rules, Situation, Aftermath, and Next Track. While not a       The opposite side will most likely be run by the GM or players
hard-and-fast rule, this progression will help a GM.                   who do not have a vested interest in the success or failure of

so the Attacker is determined to be bigger than the defending                  the players to accomplish to help move the story forward? Are
players’ Force. As such, the GM makes note that the Attacker will              they on guard duty when an attack hits? Then their objectives

OBJECTIVES                                                                     objectives might include seizing and holding a bridge, a depot, or
   While Options are usually listed before the Objectives, it’s                an airfield. Or they may be assigned a headhunter mission, where
important to sort out the track’s end goals first. Often, the more             their objective is to destroy a command and control center, or
Objectives in place, the fewer Options should be allotted due to               assassinate an enemy commander. The potential is endless.
a magnification of difficulty and the risk of overly-high rewards if              The Sample Objectives Table provides a list of more common
