   A good rule of thumb is to have at least two Objectives for each            no means exhaustive. Players should use these examples as ideas
track, though three gives a lot more tactical variances to fit all kinds       or a quick way to fill in additional objectives that fit their current
of group dynamics.                                                             track and campaign.



                                                          SAMPLE OBJECTIVES
                           WP Reward
        Objective Title     (approx.) Details
        Advance Attack        300      Destroy/Cripple at least half of the opposing Force.
        Annihilate            500      Cripple/destroy all of the opposing Force.
        Asset Denial          200      Retain control of a designated unit, building, person, or other asset at the end of the track.
        Being Difficult       400      At least two-thirds of the player’s Force must be operational at the end of the track.
        Blitzkrieg!           250      Destroy/Cripple a certain number of opposing units (half or more) by a defined time limit.
        Boom!                 500      Force the enemy to withdraw completely.
                                       Allow no more than half of the enemy Force to exit off their home edge. (Best when used with the setup
        Boxed In              400
                                       of the Force in question on the opposite side from their home edge.)
        Breakthrough          200      The players exit at least 25% of their Force through the opponent’s home edge.
        Bring the Pain        600      Destroy/Cripple at least 75% of the enemy Force.
                                       The players must complete the track without a friendly pilot death caused by any means. For example: A
        Bullet Time           100      cockpit critical hit, MechWarrior damage through failed Piloting Skill Rolls, ammo explosions, falling, and
                                       so forth.
        Burn the Flag         500      Locate and destroy the enemy Force’s headquarters unit/building/location.
                                       Pre-select no less than 30% of the opposing Force’s units before setup. Cripple or Destroy all of these
        Cherry Picker         400
                                       units. These units do not have to be revealed to the opposing player.
                                       Destroy all of the enemy Force’s specific unit types (BattleMechs, vehicles, battle armor, etc.) and hold a
        Clean House           500
                                       specific building/location at the end of the track.
        Clear the Zone        100      Destroy all the buildings on the battlefield.
        Close the Door        300      Destroy, Cripple, or Force Withdrawal at least 75% of the enemy Force.
        Command and
                              100      Destroy the opposing command post and retain a friendly command post by the end of the track.
        Conquer
                                       Stop as many vehicles as possible from exiting the battlefield. Points are given on a per-unit basis, often
        Containment            *
                                       +10, +25, or +50 depending on the track’s overall story goal.
                                       The players must not have any units destroyed through attacks into the unit’s rear arc. In other words, do
        Cover Me!             200
                                       not die from getting shot in the back!
        Cut Off the Head      100      Destroy/Cripple the enemy commander.
        Dead…with
                             1000      Destroy/Cripple all of the enemy Force before a specified time limit.
        Prejudice
        Delaying Tactics      500      Destroy/Cripple two or more commanders or officers, as designated by the GM, before a specified time limit.
                                       Keep the enemy Force from destroying a set number of buildings or CF before a set time limit or the end
        Denial                400
                                       of the track.
        Discourage                     Destroy/Cripple the opposing Force’s fastest units; one unit per four fielded is an average number. WP
                               *
        Pursuit                        awarded per unit, often +50, +75, or +100.
        Drive the Nail        300      Destroy/Cripple at least half of the enemy Force.

                                 Locate (using Scanning, which should be detailed in Special Rules) and destroy a headquarters, an enemy              MAP-BASED

                        100      commander, specific unit types, buildings, or other objective. Multiple WP can be awarded if multiple                CAMPAIGNS

                                 targets are in play during the track.

                        200      section. Higher WP can be awarded if the percentage of enemy Forces to be identified is increased (i.e.             CHAOS

                                 two-thirds, 75%, etc.).                                                                                            CAMPAIGN
Information             250      Capture or Cripple a GM-designated unit among the enemy Force.                                                     CREATION

                                 Destroy/Cripple secretly designated units or unit types; WP can be awarded as a blanket total, or per unit.           SALVAGE,
                                                                                                                                                       REPAIR &

                                                                                                                                                    CUSTOMIZATION
                                 trigger events during the track.

                                 not under Forced Withdrawal or Crippled status.

                                        SAMPLE OBJECTIVES (CONTINUED)
                   WP Reward
Objective Title     (approx.) Details
Master the Bid          *      WP awarded per unit that does not fire any weapon during the course of the track.
New Toys               50      Capture a designated unit by Crippling (and not Destroying) it.
No Witnesses          400      Destroy (not Cripple) all of the enemy’s Force.
                               Capture a designated amount of cargo tonnage, typically within a building or cargo carrier. Rules for
Pick Up               300
                               capturing should be detailed in the Special Rules section.
                               Capture (not Destroy) the enemy commander by Crippling the unit and having a unit within two hexes of
POW                   100
                               it at the end of the track.
Pre-emptive                    Destroy/Cripple at least 25% of the enemy Force by a predetermined time limit, often close to the start of
                      100
Strike                         the battle.
Preserve
                      350      Destroy/Cripple at least 50% of the enemy’s Force without losing more than 25% of the player’s Force.
Your Force
Professionals         200      Destroy/Cripple more than 75% of the enemy’s Force before reinforcements arrive at a designated time.
Reduce
                      150      Damage at least 75% of the enemy’s Force before a specified time limit.
Effectiveness
Research
                      150      Cripple a GM-designated unit and keep it from exiting the battlefield so it can be salvaged.
Junkies
Run Them Down          *       Award WP for every enemy unit Destroyed. A higher WP can be given if a designated time limit is also imposed.
Secure the Zone       500      Occupy at least 3 GM-designated locations by a designated time limit.
                               Before the start of the track, the enemy must secretly write down which of the player’s units they
Selective                      must destroy. The enemy must declare at least half of the player's Force (rounded down). For each unit
                       *
Destruction                    destroyed, the player loses a number of WP from their total. WP can also be awarded for each of the listed
                               targets survive the track.
Shatter               800      Destroy/Cripple at least 80% of the enemy’s Force.
Shatter Their                  Destroy a building/object secretly designated by the opponent. Building/object is selected before the
                      400
Will                           start of the game and is revealed at the end of the track.
Stand Your
                      250      At least half of the player’s Force survives the end of the track.
Ground
Stop Them Cold         *       WP are lost for every enemy unit that exits via their home edge that are not under Forced Withdrawal rules.
Superior                       Destroy/Cripple the units of the two best enemy pilots or crews as determined by skill. If there are more
                      200
Firepower                      than two, the enemy may select which two pilots for the objective.
Sustainability        100      At least 25% of a third party Force assisting the players survives the track.
Symbolic Victory      200      At least one player unit occupies a GM designated hex at the end of the track or by a certain time limit.
                               The player must seize an enemy-designated objective hex and occupy it for a set number of Turns (can be
                               consecutive or non-sequential and be the same unit or a combination of units, as determined by the GM
Take the Hill!        350
                               according to those that best fit the track story). Multiple objectives can be awarded WP per completion,
                               or a minimum number of objectives set before a blanket award is given.
Target Acquired       100      Destroy a set number of buildings/locations by a set time limit.
The Road to
                      500      Destroy, drive off, or capture a set percentage of the opposing Force by a set time limit.
Paradise
Total Victory         200      Complete all other Objectives. Add +100 if done while a track Option is in effect.
Training Day          300      At least one third-party Force that is allied with the player must suffer no more than 25% casualties.
                               Destroy as much of the enemy Force as possible. WP offered per unit Destroyed, typically +50, +100,
Turkey Shoot           *
                               +200 depending on needs of the campaign.
Undercut              300      Destroy/Cripple 100% of all third party Forces allied with the opponent.
                               Destroy/Cripple 100% of the enemy Force while taking less than a certain percentage of damage, often
Vengeance             500
                               25% or 50%.
World of Hurt         500      Destroy/Cripple at least two-thirds of the enemy Force.

VICTORY CONDITIONS (OPTIONAL)
   One variation to the Warchest Objectives is to assign the WP
reward to the number of Objectives completed, rather than giving
                                                                                       OPTIONS                                               INNER SPHERE
                                                                                                                                             SOLAR SYSTEM
                                                                                                                                                AT WAR
                                                                                                                                              GENERATION
                                                                                                                                             CONVERSIONS
set WP for each. This is a simpler system that allows players who         While the final track setup has these listed before the
are not playing the campaign for tactical exercise, but more in the    Objectives, it is good practice for a GM to wait until they
“just have fun” dynamic. By having a few tiers in place, players can   have most of the track completed before deciding on what
mix and match Objective completion on the fly, adjusting for the                                                                                 LINKED
                                                                       Options they wish to offer. This is because Options will
                                                                                                                                               SCENARIOS
vagaries that often happen on the tabletop battlefield.                have an overall effect on the entire track, and may skew the
   The GM assigns between three and five Objectives to                 difficulty against the players. Rewards should be scaled with
the track without a listed WP award. Next, build the Victory           the challenge; Option WP rewards are given if just one of the
Condition hierarchy. Select each level of hierarchy that matches       player Objectives is met.                                               NARRATIVE
the number of Objectives assigned to the track.                           The Options Table presents a small sampling of the Options           CAMPAIGNS
                                                                       that can be applied to a track. WP cited are a suggested range;
                                                                       the GM is encouraged to change them to a reward that fits their
       VICTORY CONDITIONS HIERARCHY                                    group, the track, and the overall story campaign. Additionally,         MAP-BASED
                                                                       the GM should feel free to create customized Options that fit           CAMPAIGNS
    Condition          Definition                                      the story their track is telling, adding atmosphere, difficulty, or
    Total Victory      Complete all Objectives.                        even new story elements.
    Clear Victory      Complete all but one Objective.                    Note that a GM should not just randomly assign Options              CHAOS
                                                                                                                                             CAMPAIGN
                       Complete only half of the Objectives            to their track. Options are meant to enhance the story
    Partial Victory                                                                                                                          CREATION
                       (rounding up).                                  atmosphere of the track, adding variables and challenges
    Pyrrhic Victory
                       Complete less than half of the                  that make sense to the particular situation. Examine each
                       Objectives, but more than one.                                                                                         MAINTENANCE,
                                                                       Option against the “spirit” of the track—does it fit the                 SALVAGE,
    Partial Defeat     Complete only one Objective.                    story goals the GM seeks? Does it add more detail to the                 REPAIR &
    Total Defeat       No objectives completed.                        player experience? If all of the Options, or any combination          CUSTOMIZATION
                                                                       thereof, are used and the players achieve their Objectives,
                                                                       are the rewards balanced? Nothing is more overbalanced
   Assign WP to each condition. As with Objectives, the WP cost                                                                                 RECORD
                                                                       than offering an Option that has no bearing or effect on the
                                                                                                                                                SHEETS
is ultimately determined by the GM to fit within the campaign          actual track, such as applying “High Speed Interception” to
framework. Ideally, the Total Victory condition should be a sum        a ground-based game.
total of each individual Objective’s presumed WP award, and               Options can easily be applied instead as a Special Rule;
break down from there. Players can only achieve one victory            these affect the track regardless and cannot be chosen, unlike
condition; it is not a sum total of all conditions reached.            standard Options.

                                                                                                                                                                INTRODUCTION

                               All hexes cost an extra 1 MP to enter. All Piloting Skill Rolls are given a penalty of 1 due to bad footing with the
Sand                25-100
                               shifting sand (see p. 37, TO:AR or p. 60, AS:CE).
Sensor
                     25-75     Use the Electromagnetic Interference rules (see p. 53, TO:AR or p. 62, AS:CE).                                                      FORCE

                                                                                                                                                                  CREATION
                               Roll 2D6 for the first clear hex each ’Mech enters during a turn while using Running MP. If the roll is a 10 or
                               greater, a sub-Level 1 sinkhole opens beneath the unit. If the clear hex is next to an existing sinkhole, a new
Sinkholes           200-450
                               sinkhole will open on a roll of 8 or greater. The player must then make a Piloting Skill Roll with a +1 modifier for
                               that unit to avoid falling into the hole. Players should mark that hex for future reference.                                        FORCE
                               Apply a +2 to-hit modifier to all missile weapon attacks and a +1 to-hit modifier to all direct-fire ballistic                    OPERATIONS
                               weapon attacks. Apply a +1 modifier to all Piloting Skill Rolls. The following restrictions also apply:
                               Airships: Apply a –1 modifier to Safe Thrust and a +3 modifier to all Control Rolls.
                               Hover, WiGE and VTOLs: Apply a +2 modifier to all Piloting Skill Rolls.
                               Aerospace units (except for Airships): Apply a +1 modifier to all Control Rolls.                                                  FORMATION
Strong Gale         600-750                                                                                                                                       BUILDING
                               Battle Armor: Apply –1 Ground MP to all battle armor, to a minimum of 0; any such units can either move or

                               Conventional Infantry: Apply –2 Ground MP to all conventional infantry, to a minimum of 0; any units reduced
                               to 0 MP can either move or make a weapon attack in a turn, but not both. No jumping movement allowed.                            INNER SPHERE
                               (Alpha Strike: Use Wind Force 3, p. 63, AS:CE.)                                                                                     AT WAR

                                                                       WARCHEST
                          WP AWARDS                               i
                                                                          Once the Objectives and Options have been decided, it’s time
                                                                       to sort out the “cost” of the track and what its rewards will be. An
  There is no hard-and-fast rule for determining Warchest              easy method is to simply add up all of the Objectives and Options
Point awards for Options or Objectives. Ultimately it is the GM’s      rewards as if the players would succeed at all of them, and then
decision and should factor in a number of variables:                   make the track cost 75 percent of that total. The main downside
  • How difficult is the objective to complete?                        to this method is that if the players succeed, but don’t achieve all
  • How difficult does the Option make the battlefield for             of the offered Objectives and Options, the players’ victory may not
     units to function?                                                cover the cost of the track. This is usually acceptable for tracks in the
  • How many points is acceptable as a reward, assuming the            first half of the campaign, or if it fits the overall mood of the story
     player selects all Options and wins all Objectives?               the GM is trying to tell. However, later tracks should have larger
  • How crucial is the track to the overall campaign? More             rewards over the track cost, to account for more intense combat
     points should be awarded if the track is a lynchpin or key        situations as the climax of the campaign unwinds.
     mission; “side trip” tracks should award less, but still enough      One of the key points to proper rewards is to balance the payoff
     to provide the player a buffer or extra in case of need—          versus where the player group is in the campaign, how much
     assuming they can complete some or all of the Objectives.         material and technology they have, and how much fun the group
  • Where is the track located in the overall campaign structure?      is experiencing. Lower payouts at the start of the campaign limits
     Typically, fewer points are awarded for tracks that occur in      the risk of a “Monty Haul” situation. Nothing is more painful for
     the first half of the campaign, with larger payouts towards       a GM—and boring for a player—than having too much power,
     the end. This is in part to help the players recover after a      skills, and gear early on so that they plow through the rest of the
     brutal campaign, reinforcing and/or repairing equipment           campaign with no sense of danger or difficulty.
     and setting up for their next campaign.                              Conversely, if a difficult track is crafted—often found in the middle
  Successfully addressing these questions gives the GM a               or near the end of the campaign—the GM is encouraged to make the
better idea on how to assign WP awards.                                payout larger than normal, to give players a sense of victory. The WP
                                                                       reward is one of the best ways to help control the flow and type of
                                                                       campaign. Consistent, low-paying rewards gives the group a sense of
                                                                       “on the edge of bankruptcy,” where one bad mission could sink a unit.
                             SALVAGE                              i
                                                                       This often fits a group prone to over-preparation and caution, who
                                                                       plan as much as possible for every event. Conversely, consistently
    Salvage is the taking of destroyed or crippled equipment           high-paying rewards can provide a group that is more interested in
remaining on the battlefield at the end of a track. In linked          playing with new technology or strategies, and don’t mind burning
sce­narios, salvage may not occur until the end of the mini-           through equipment and material in order to claim victory. GMs who
campaign unless the players’ total Force includes units noted          know their groups can tailor WP accordingly.
for salvage operations and/or has lift hoists, at the agreement           For the purposes of these guidelines, it’s assumed that the GM
of all players involved.                                               is constructing tracks for a typical wargaming group. Therefore,
    For Chaos Campaign tracks, and unless a track’s Special Rules      rewards will be in the 10-25 percent range for normal difficulty.
state otherwise, players who achieve at least one Objective in
a track may claim all battlefield salvage in any area where their      SPECIAL RULES
units participated. Players may not claim salvage after battles           After Options and Objectives are determined and the cost of the
in which their Forces did not participate.                             track is decided, the Special Rules that will govern the track are put
    Salvage may be sold at half the unit’s normal list price           into place. Note that tracks are not required to have Special Rules;
using the GM’s determined tables or resources for Support              for simplistic tracks that contain standard Objectives and Options,
Points. Alternatively, vehicles, ’Mechs, and other salvaged units      they are often not needed.
may be repaired either by using the Warchest system or by                 As a rule of thumb, Special Rules are designed to add atmosphere,
converting Support Points to C-bills and using an appropriate          a unique twist, or flavor to the track; often, these rules will affect
rule set to spend C-bills for repairing or replacing equipment.        only one side or the other. If a particular side is not indicated in
    Players interested in more detailed Salvage rules can find         the description, then it is applied to all units participating in the
them beginning on page 214.                                            track, regardless of side.
    In some instances, the GM can determine that salvage is               A GM should examine the Objectives and Options they have put into
unavailable, due to time constraints within the campaign or            place; most of the time, these will determine what, if any, Special Rules
between tracks. If the Salvage rule is in overall effect (where        are applicable. For example, if one of the Objectives calls for successful
it is automatically awarded between tracks), the GM should             scanning, then rules for this should be defined in the Special Rules
state in a track’s Special Rules if it is unavailable at that time,    section. If there are particular rules from Tactical Operations, Strategic
or if other limitations are in play.                                   Operations, or Interstellar Operations that are required to run the track,
                                                                       those should be referenced in this section as well.
    There are two fundamental Special Rules that are often                          GMs can put as many Special Rules into play as they desire,
considered in play when it comes to Chaos Campaign tracks:                       though it is recommended to limit them to only those necessary
Forced Withdrawal and Salvage. The Forced Withdrawal rules                       to complete any Objectives or Options that may be selected.
are found in Total Warfare as optional rules. GMs who wish to                    A GM can also include special unit rules, if either the player or
have this rule in effect can lay out which units must operate by                 the opponent (or both) are using a canon unit that has such
                                                                                                                                                                INTRODUCTION
it; this can be all units, just the player or opponent, or defined               rules published.
even further by stipulating exact Formations or specific units.                     The Special Rules Table highlights only a small sample of
    Salvage is a rule that is usually defined at the start of the                various rules that could be utilized in a campaign track. If the
campaign and considered active for every track. However,                         GM sees a Special Rule they would like to use, it is best if they                 FORCE
GMs can also stipulate only certain tracks that are subject                      formulate an Option or Objective that is reliant on the rule for                 CREATION
to it, and can even predetermine the amount of salvage                           completion. Special Rules can also be adapted and modified
available, if desired.                                                           into Options or even Objectives, at the GM’s discretion.

                                                          SPECIAL RULES TABLE                                                                                    OPERATIONS


    Rule Title             Description
                           Place one AA gun for each aerospace unit fielded by the Attacker. Each emplacement is a Level 2 building with a CF of 30              FORMATION
    AA Guns                and contains one LB 5-X autocannon on a 360-degree swivel mount. Each gun has 10 rounds of ammunition. AA guns can                     BUILDING
                           also fire on ground targets with a +2 To-Hit modifier.
                           Aerospace units on both sides have access to time-delayed bombs, which function as bombs for purposes of rules and damages,
    Aerospace                                                                                                                                                   INNER SPHERE
                           with the option of how deep the bomb may go underwater before exploding. Depth times may be changed during the End
    Bomb Racks                                                                                                                                                     AT WAR
                           Phase. If carrying split loads of ordinance, players should note which racks carry which bombs on their record sheets.
                           Hidden units may make indirect attacks without a spotter with an additional +1 to-hit penalty and do not reveal                      CONVERSIONS
    Ambush
                           themselves when making an indirect attack.
                           (Space only.) Add 2D6-2 asteroids or large debris in the battlefield (see pp. 36-37, SO). Roll 1D6; a result of 6 indicates a       INNER SPHERE
    Asteroids/Debris                                                                                                                                           SOLAR SYSTEM
                                                                                                                                                                  AT WAR
                           large piece of debris instead of an asteroid. For each object, roll 1D6. On a result of 3-6, the object is mobile.
                           With nowhere left to run the opponents are capable of fighting with greater levels of desperation and fervor. All                    GENERATION
                                                                                                                                                               CONVERSIONS
    Backs to the Wall      BattleMech physical attacks are made with a +1 to-hit modifier and all Defender MechWarriors add +1 to their
                           Consciousness Checks. However, no defending unit may voluntarily leave the map.
    Battlefield Morale     If using multiple tracks for the assault, every track that is successfully completed grants the player a +1 Initiative modifier.        LINKED
                           Up to half of the player’s Force may betray their fellow warriors during a track. At the End Phase of Turn 5, roll 2D6 for each       SCENARIOS
    Betrayal               unit. If the result is 10+, that unit switches to the opponent's side with the Initiative Phase of the next turn. The roll can be
                           modified using a GM-created modifier table.
    Biohazard Alert        All Infantry units and unsealed Vehicles suffer –1 MP due to the biohazard protocols requiring chemical suits.
                                                                                                                                                                 NARRATIVE
                           The attack comes just before the bridges are rigged to blow. To determine when the explosives are ready, the Defender rolls
                                                                                                                                                                 CAMPAIGNS
                           2D6+5; during the End Phase of this and subsequent turns, the Defender may detonate one non-tower bridge hex for each
    Blow the Bridges
                           combat engineer unit surviving, to a maximum of two per turn. Units on a detonating hex suffer a strike similar to a 20-point
                           conventional minefield (see p. 176, TO:AR or Density 3 Minefield p. 168, AS:CE) then a one-Level fall into the water below.
                           The buildings are booby-trapped to explode if a unit gets too close or lingers too long near it. During the End Phase,                MAP-BASED
                           determine the number of enemy units within a two-hex radius of a building. If there is at least one unit, roll 2D6; on a result       CAMPAIGNS
    Booby-trap Buildings   of 11-12, the building explodes, causing 20 points of damage to units in the same hex, 10 points of damage to adjacent units,
                           and 5 points of damage to units 2 hexes away. Damage is applied in 5-point Damage Value groupings. For every unit above
                           two within proximity, add a +1 modifier to the roll. (Alpha Strike damage is as per an HE Bomb, p. 183, AS:CE.)                      CHAOS
                           In order to capture goods from a target hex, the player must spend a full round with a unit in that hex (not counting the round     CAMPAIGN
    Capturing Goods        the unit enters or exits the hex). While a unit is spending the round “capturing goods,” it may not make any weapon attacks, but    CREATION
                           is not considered immobile. Once the full round is complete, the unit is considered to have somehow lashed or stolen the goods.
                           Once a unit has captured goods, it must immediately withdraw per the normal Forced Withdrawal rules. If they are                     MAINTENANCE,
                           destroyed while leaving, another unit must enter the destroyed unit's hex to pick up the goods as before.                              SALVAGE,
                           Use the Castles Brian Complexes rules (see p. 139, TO:AR) when constructing the Castles Brian. It may contain structures up            REPAIR &
    Castle Brian           to 4 Levels above or below the map surface, and no more than 12 fixed weapon emplacements. At least 3 turrets must be               CUSTOMIZATION
                           dedicated AA platforms.
                           In order to get a vehicle to stop, an attacking unit must move within one hex (or 50 meters) of a civilian unit and call for that
                           unit’s surrender. During the End Phase, roll 1D6 for that civilian vehicle. On a result of 1 to 4, the unit ignores the command.       RECORD
                           On a 5 or 6, the unit surrenders. The player may modify the roll by hitting the vehicle with a non-lethal attack (any attack that      SHEETS
                           causes damage of 3 points or less), which adds a +1 modifier. For each successive attempt, add +1 to the roll.
    Civilian Vehicles
                           Civilian vehicles may be any unit that has a motive system of Airship, VTOL, Hover, Wheeled, Tracked, or Fixed Wing. If in
                           space, then vehicles can be any DropShip, Small Craft, or Aerospace fighter.
                           Units may only demand the surrender of one civilian unit per turn. Civilian vehicles that exit the battlefield are
                           considered escaped.



                                                                                                                                                                   179
                                       SPECIAL RULES TABLE (CONTINUED)
Rule Title           Description
                     If a player is using a Clan Force, it must follow Clan Honor rules (see p. 273, TW). Forces that are not Clan in nature begin
                     the track with every unit receiving 2 dezgra points. Players may choose to maintain a record of dezgra points for their unit
Clan Honor           throughout the campaign, but it is not required. If dezgra points are not tracked for a campaign, they are reset to zero at
                     the start of each track.
                     Opponents using a Clan Force must also follow Clan Honor rules and keep record of dezgra points for the track.
                     A Command Center confers bonuses to the Force that controls them. Each building is considered to have an inherit
                     Guardian ECM and allows the controller to move two units at the end of the Movement Phase, after all other Forces on
                     the battlefield have moved. If a Center is lost through destruction, the controlling player at the time automatically loses
                     Initiative for the next three turns. If both sides lose a Command Center, determine Initiative as normal.
Command Center       In order to take control of a command center, an operational unit (including units that are Crippled but not Destroyed)
                     must remain in an adjacent hex (infantry may occupy the hex) for at least 3 uncontested turns. If an enemy unit is
                     adjacent to the building, the turn does not count towards the 3 turns needed to seize control.
                     Once controlled, the Center’s bonus transfers to its new owner at the start of the next turn. The track starts with the
                     Command Center under the Defender’s control.
                     Both the Attacker and Defender have the opportunity to use both a Command Officer and a Company Command
                     Officer. Both of these Officers have a Piloting and Gunnery Skill Rating 1 better than the Force of which they are a part.
Command Officers
                     In addition, if a player has both Officers fielded due to having enough units, then the Command Officer gains a random
                     Special Pilot Ability (see pp. 71-72).
                     Treat the locations of these posts as normal Hidden units (non-infantry) for purposes of discovery. Once discovered,
Command Posts        each location has a CF of 60 for the purposes of destruction, though it is not considered a building. (Buildings placed/
                     designated as such are actually artificial facades and will collapse with any weight on them.)
                     One of the units on each side is a field commander. Select one unit and assign it a Skill Rating one better than the Force’s
Commander            average. If the unit containing the commander is destroyed, that player must apply a –2 modifier to all subsequent
                     Initiative rolls.
                     The opponent selects one map; the chosen map will have modified hexes. All Clear hexes that do not have another terrain
Crops
                     feature on the chosen map are considered Planted Fields (see p. 36, TO:AR or p. 60, AS:CE).
                     Any chance of pursuit must be destroyed. In order to accomplish this, the Attacker must do the following:
                     All opposing units capable of jump movement must be disabled or destroyed.
Disable Pursuit
                     All opposing VTOL units must be disabled or destroyed.
                     All opposing units capable of faster movement than the Force’s current fastest unit must be disabled or destroyed.
                     If both players agree, the Attacker should roll 1D6; on a result of 4 or higher, both players may choose one champion each
Duel                 from the Forces selected for battle to fight a duel. Whichever player destroys the other’s unit first wins and receives the
                     Rewards for the track’s Objectives.
                     If a Force is reduced to a point at which a Forced Withdrawal is called for, that unit instead applies a –1 modifier to all
Fanatical Devotion   Gunnery and Piloting Skills and will fight until destroyed or unable to function; that Force does not follow the normal
                     Forced Withdrawal rules at any time thereafter.
                     The Defender builds a mid-sized defensive fortification on the battlefield using the Advanced Building Construction rules
Firebase
                     (see p. 126, TO:AR), and sets up the fortification after Force selection but before the start of the track.
                     A nearby dam has broken due to excessive nearby combat and is flooding the plains around it. Beginning in Turn 3,
                     expand all rivers present by two hexes on each bank (so a one-hex river becomes a five-hex river); the new hexes are at
                     Depth 1. Every odd turn thereafter, increase the river size by two hexes, with the new hexes at Depth 1 and all prior water
                     hexes increasing in depth by one.
Floodwaters
                     All terrain Level 1 and higher remains above water; the water is spreading, not rising, due to the soil erosion occurring
                     underneath the surface of the water. All woods hexes that are covered by water are cleared.
                     All water hexes are affected by the Torrent rules (see p. 50, TO:AR). The direction of water flow is determined by the
                     Defender at the beginning of Turn 3.
Forced Withdrawal    The Forced Withdrawal rules (see p. 258, TW or p. 126, AS:CE) are in effect.
                     Up to half the units on each side may turn traitor at some point in the battle. Beginning on Turn 2, roll 2D6 for each
                     functional unit; on a result of 10+, that unit switches sides. Once a unit has switched sides, that unit makes no more rolls.
Free-fire Zone
                     Continue this search during each Initiative Phase until half of a player’s units have switched sides, or Turn 5 is reached. This
                     effect lasts through the end of the battle.
                     Radio frequency jamming by the opponent prevents communications until Turn 6, during which time the players suffer a
Frequency Overload
                     –1 modifier to all Initiative rolls.
                     The static fortifications are considered single-hex, Level 1 Medium Buildings with a CF of 40 and an Armor Factor of 40.
                     Each building can have a turret-mounted Large Laser, a turret mounted LRM 20 with two tons of ammo, an LB 10-X AC
Gun Emplacements
                     with two tons of ammo, or any other mix of weapons as determined by the GM. The gunnery crews have a Gunnery Skill
                     Rating also determined by the GM.
                                         SPECIAL RULES TABLE (CONTINUED)
Rule Title             Description
                       The opponents (or all sides) may use Orbit-to-Surface Fire (see p. 91, SO or p. 84, ASC) using a GM-determined WarShip or
Hand of God                                                                                                                                              INTRODUCTION
                       DropShip; the GM should place a limit on the number of salvos available.
Industrial Nightmare   Treat 3 hexes around each Hardened Building as a Heavy Industrial Zone (see p. 29, TO:AR or p. 58, AS:CE).
                       Excluding conventional infantry, units may pass through Irradiated hexes without penalty. Any unit ending its movement
                       in an Irradiated terrain hex suffers the following effects:
                                                                                                                                                            FORCE
                       Add a +1 to-hit modifier for any weapon attacks due to lingering electromagnetic interference and radiation.
                                                                                                                                                           CREATION
                       Add 5 heat during the Heat Phase (if applicable).
                       ‘Mechs: For every two consecutive turns spent in Irradiated terrain, the unit’s MechWarrior takes one point of pilot
                       damage during the End Phase of the second turn.
Irradiated Terrain     Ground vehicles: For every two consecutive turns spent in Irradiated terrain, the unit suffers the following critical hit            FORCE
                       during the End Phase of the indicated turn (see p. 194, TW): Crew Stunned (2nd turn), Commander Hit (4th turn), Crew               OPERATIONS
                       Killed (6th turn).
                       Battle armor: For every two consecutive turns spent in Irradiated terrain, the unit loses 1 randomly selected trooper
                       during the End Phase of the second turn.
                       Conventional infantry: Conventional infantry units may neither move through nor end their movement in an Irradiated                FORMATION
                       hex. If a conventional infantry unit is forced into an Irradiated hex, it is considered destroyed.                                  BUILDING
                       A Clan Force may claim all non-destroyed enemy units remaining on the battlefield at the end of the track as isorla. Players
                       claiming isorla must have successfully completed at least two Objectives and used at least one Option in the track.
                       A pilot claimed as isorla is a bondsman and may be utilized by the Clan player in a future battle. A bondsman fights as           INNER SPHERE
                       if his Skill Rating is one less than listed. A bondsman must earn their way back to warrior status with their new Clan; for          AT WAR
                       these rules, this is achieved by winning three duels as a bondsman. [NOTE: Clan Blood Spirit and Clan Steel Viper warriors        CONVERSIONS
                       taken as bondsmen must win five duels before they can be accepted.] A bondsman who succeeds may join the Clan as
Isorla
                       abtakha and regains their previous Skill Rating. Kills and other points towards advancement, if used, are not counted
                                                                                                                                                        INNER SPHERE
                       during the warrior’s time as a bondsman.
                                                                                                                                                        SOLAR SYSTEM
                                                                                                                                                           AT WAR
                       Players are not required to take enemy pilots as isorla; those not claimed are considered executed.
                                                                                                                                                         GENERATION
                                                                                                                                                        CONVERSIONS
                       If the track is played by two competing players, the winner is determined by the highest WP reward total and denies the
                       other side isorla or salvage.
                       Equipment taken as isorla may be repaired.
                       Each side is made up of untried warriors. Unless within 5 hexes of a Lance or Star CO, normal units will act as follows:             LINKED
                       Inner Sphere: Inner Sphere units that destroy or cripple an enemy unit will continue to target and fire upon that unit             SCENARIOS
Lack of Discipline     until its Head or Center Torso Location Internal Structure is completely destroyed.
                       Clan: Add a +1 To-hit modifier to all targets that use a minimum of 1 MP in the turn being targeted.
                       Alpha Strike: Unless within 6” of their Formation or Force CO, units will act as if one Experience/Skill Rating worse.
                       The buildings are considered to be part of a C3 network; three turrets have fire control computers that function exactly           NARRATIVE
                       like a C3 Slave Module; the master control tower functions exactly like a C3 Master Computer. When attacking, each turret          CAMPAIGNS
Live Fire Training     is considered to have a Gunnery of 5. The turrets are part of an automated network and automatically attack the closest
                       unit (Attacker or Defender) within range and LOS of each turret. When multiple targets are present, the turret attacks the
                       heaviest target; in the case of ties, roll randomly to determine which unit to attack.
                                                                                                                                                          MAP-BASED
                       Out of conventional mines, the Defenders resort to unused missiles and artillery shells to create their minefields, making         CAMPAIGNS
Makeshift Minefield    their power and sensitivity an unknown quantity. When a minefield explodes, it does 3D6 damage (AS: 1D6 divided by 2)
                       in that hex, and half the amount of rolled damage to units in the adjacent hex. Location is determined normally.
                       Set up the monorail line as a 1-hex-wide row of Rail terrain type (see p. 49, TO:AR or p. 60, AS:CE) from one edge of the
                                                                                                                                                         CHAOS
                       map to the opposite edge. The rail can follow terrain features as necessary and cannot bridge sublevel hexes. The rail line
                                                                                                                                                        CAMPAIGN
Monorail               itself is fortified and slightly elevated; treat those hexes as half-levels (see p. 29, TO:AR) and as having a CF of 60 rather
                                                                                                                                                        CREATION
                       than the standard CF 20 for Rail terrain. Any hex of the rail line that is destroyed becomes Ultra Rubble (see p. 37, TO:AR or
                       p. 60, AS:CE). This Special Rule can be combined with Pipelines (see p. 182).
                                                                                                                                                         MAINTENANCE,
Morale                 Both sides use the in-game Morale rules (see p. 179, TO:AR or p. 169, AS:CE).
                                                                                                                                                           SALVAGE,
No Holds Barred        As long as the designated command unit is present, no friendly unit may be forced to withdraw.                                      REPAIR &
                       Both Forces may assign up to half its Force with Off-Map Movement orders (see p. 85 or p. 106, AS:CE). This may only             CUSTOMIZATION
Off-Map Movement
                       occur once during the track.
                       Some missions require objectives to be completed within a short amount of time. This special rule helps reflect that
                       battlefield condition.                                                                                                              RECORD
                       The player establishes a set number of turns to accomplish each Objective and write these down before the start of the              SHEETS
Operational Timeline
                       track. They do not need to be shared with the player’s opponent. If the Objective is completed in the time estimated, the
                       player receives the reward listed. For each turn under the established limit, the reward is increased by 25%. For each turn
                       past the established limit, the reward is diminished by 25%.




                                                                                                                                                            181
                                      SPECIAL RULES TABLE (CONTINUED)
Rule Title          Description
                    Commonplace in most industrialized areas or across vast expanses of open terrain, the GM designates one or more
                    hexrows as containing a pipeline; these have no effect on unit Movement. Any missed shot within a hex containing a
                    pipeline has a chance to rupture the line. A result of 9 or higher on a 2D6 roll indicates a pipeline was struck. The pipelines
                    have CF 30 and contain a variety of interesting liquids. If a pipeline takes damage equivalent to its CF, it ruptures and
                    spews its contents onto the battlefield. Roll 1D6 to determine those contents: on a result of 1-3, the pipeline contains
                    water; on a 4-5, it contains oil; and on a result of 6, it contains hazardous chemicals. For ease of gameplay, each hex of the
                    line can only have a single breach at a time.
                    Once a pipeline has ruptured, liquid begins pouring out onto the ground. Any BattleMech unit in the hex immediately
                    in front of the rupture must make a successful Piloting Skill Roll to avoid being knocked down by the pressurized stream
                    of liquid and displaced one hex. Vehicle units must make a successful Driving Skill Roll to avoid being displaced one hex.
                    Infantry units are automatically displaced one hex by the torrent of liquid. All units receive 20 points of damage, divided
Pipelines           into 5-point groups and randomly distributed.
                    Starting with the hex immediately in front of the rupture, all affected hexes receive the Mud terrain modification (see
                    p. 48, TO:AR or p. 60, AS:CE) for the remainder of the track. In addition, oil and hazardous chemical spills also have the
                    following effects:
                    Oil: Apply a –3 modifier to rolls made to determine if a fire starts in an affected hex.
                    Hazardous chemicals: Use the Hazardous Liquid Pools rules (see p. 47, TO:AR or p. 58, AS:CE).
                    If a hex contains a spill of two or more types (if, for instance, two breaches spread into contact), the later spill’s effects
                    supersede the prior. If both spills spread into the hex at the same time, determine randomly which one takes precedence.
                    Each turn that a pipeline spews out liquid, expand the spill one ring of hexes. A pipeline will continue to spill for 5 Turns
                    before safety measures kick in and seal the breach. Once this happens, the breached hex is eligible for further breaches
                    as per the above rules. If an infantry unit is present on the rail line in the same hex and spends 2 Turns without moving or
                    attacking, the spill will end after those two Turns as they manage to manually activate the shutoff mechanisms.
                    The opponent can place a GM-determined number of prepared defenses on the battlefield, represented by a counter.
                    These defenses function as Heavy Buildings with CF 25; attacks against defending units in these hexes must target the
Prepared Defenses
                    Prepared Defense, just as targeting infantry in a building must target the building. However, reduce the damage passed
                    on to the target by half the normal amount.
                    Prior to the start of the battle, each unit on both sides receives 3D6 damage (Alpha Strike: 1D6), distributed normally in
Prior Damage
                    5-point groups. Reroll any damage that would result in a Crippled or Destroyed unit.
                    Any attacking BattleMech unit with undamaged Sensors can make a dedicated radio band scan once every two turns.
Radio Emissions     Scans are declared and resolved in the End Phase. When scanning, roll 2D6; on a result of 11 or 12, the location of a
                    targeted or objective unit is revealed.
                    In a battle deemed so important, both sides find themselves being reinforced by various independent Forces as
                    commanders desperately try to sway the outcome.
                    Starting on a turn designated by the GM, each side rolls 2D6 before the Initiative Phase. On a 10+, that side receives
                    reinforcements equal to 25% of their original Force. These reinforcements may be banked for future entrance into the battle.
Relief Forces
                    Once reinforcements are rolled as available, no more rolls are made. For every turn after Turn 3, add +1 to the rolled result.
                    Units may enter one at a time, in small groups or all at once; when a player wishes to use their reinforcements, they must
                    declare it during an End Phase when reinforcements are available. Declared units will then enter the battlefield during the
                    End Phase of the following turn either through the player’s home edge or via a combat drop (see p. 20, SO or p. 160, AS:CE).
                    If a firing unit is adjacent to a hex that is at least one level higher than the unit’s occupying hex and it fires a large ballistic
                    or missile weapon (one that does a possible 15 or more damage), there is a chance to trigger a rockslide. During the End
Rockslides
                    Phase, roll 2D6; a rockslide occurs on a result of a 9 or better. The rockslide does 10 points of damage (1 point in AS) for
                    every level difference between the target and originating hex. Apply damage in 5-point groups to the unit’s front facing.
                    (Space or Aerospace only.) All units that are not small craft must enter the board with a starting Velocity of 4 or less and
                    are considered “running silent.” These craft may use up to half of its Thrust rating for maneuvering; if the unit uses more
Running Silent
                    than half of its Thrust, its Maximum Thrust rating, accelerates or decelerates, the unit is no longer considered silent and is
                    immediately detected by the opposing units and may be targeted and fired on as normal.
                    Buildings may be scanned by units without an Active Probe; such units must end their movement within 2 hexes of the
                    target building and may not fire that turn. Units with an Active Probe must end their movement within 4 hexes of the
                    target building and may fire during the same turn as scanning.
Scanning            A scan is declared during the Weapon Attack Phase and takes place during the End Phase of the same turn, provided the unit
                    remains functioning and does not have any sensor critical hits. Only BattleMechs and vehicles may perform a sensor scan.
                    Battle armor and infantry may conduct an interior search instead, using the Infantry in Building rules (see pp. 167-170,
                    TW). The unit must begin and end their turn in the same building hex in order to conduct a proper search.
                    In order to consider an objective scouted, a unit must spend a full round in that objectives’ hex, in other words, the round
Scouting            in which the unit enters the hex does not count. While a unit is spending the round “scouting,” the unit may otherwise act
                    normally and is not considered immobile.
                                     SPECIAL RULES TABLE (CONTINUED)
Rule Title          Description
                    In order to take control of a building, an operational unit (including units that are Crippled but not Destroyed) must
                                                                                                                                                     INTRODUCTION
                    remain in an adjacent hex for at least 5 turns. If any enemy units are adjacent to the same building during the End Phase
Seizing Buildings   of a turn, that turn will not count towards the total. Units do not need to be in contact the entire time, but the player’s
                    Force must maintain uncontested contact for a total of 5 full turns.
                    Destroying a building has no effect on seizing the location for Objective purposes.
                    Each side has a pool of reinforcements it can call upon in an attempt to sway the battle in their favor. During the                 FORCE
                    Initiative Phase of every fifth turn, each side may choose to call in one of their support options; these units will               CREATION
                    arrive at the start of the following turn. Reinforcements may only be called in once during the battle. These Forces
                    are selected independently of the player’s starting Force; it is recommended that players select and set aside their
                    reinforcements before beginning play.
                                                                                                                                                        FORCE
                    The player calling in reinforcements may choose two of the following three options.
                                                                                                                                                      OPERATIONS
Selective           Ground: Ground Forces equal to 25% of their starting deployed Force enters their home edge at the start of the next turn.
Reinforcements      Artillery: One off-board Sniper tube for every four units the controlling player has initially deployed (round down) is
                    available to fire at the start of the next turn. Each tube has one ton of ammunition and is considered eight boards away
                    from the centermost mapboard. The Sniper’s crew has a Gunnery Skill of 4. (Clan crews are considered to have a Gunnery            FORMATION
                    Skill of 5 and are only three boards away from the centermost mapboard.)                                                           BUILDING
                    Air: A combination of Aerospace Fighters, Conventional Fighters, and/or Small Craft equal to 20% of the starting
                    deployed Force enters the battlefield from the controlling player’s home edge starting the next turn at a Velocity of 6
                    or less (if applicable).
                                                                                                                                                     INNER SPHERE
Shielding           Both sides are using the Shielding rules (see p. 17, TO:AR or p. 172, AS:CE) when an enemy unit targets the Command unit.           AT WAR
                    The GM predetermines which Turn the track ends; this can be announced to one side, both sides, or remain hidden from             CONVERSIONS
Time Limit          all Forces. The GM announces before the Initiative Phase of the penultimate Turn; the track finishes at the conclusion of
                    the following Turn’s End Phase.
                                                                                                                                                    INNER SPHERE
                    Grasslands can easily become a flaming inferno. Any missed weapons fire has a chance of igniting the grass and starting a       SOLAR SYSTEM
                                                                                                                                                       AT WAR
Tinderbox
                    fire. Use the rules for Accidental Fires (see p. 41, TO:AR or p. 166, AS:CE).                                                    GENERATION
                                                                                                                                                    CONVERSIONS
                    The Defender receives 1 Trap per tunnel intersection, to a maximum of 12. The traps are of specific types, and the
                    defending player must record the location of Traps prior to the start of the track. The total number of traps must be
                    agreed to by both players before any are determined. The Defender can place the traps in any hex as desired. Traps are
                    set off when an attacking unit enters the hex, except in cases when the trap was successfully detected. The type of traps           LINKED
                    should be randomly determined from the following by rolling 1D6:                                                                  SCENARIOS
                    1-2: Tunnel Collapse. A Tunnel Collapse brings down the roof in the hex, permanently blocking the tunnel. Any infantry
                    unit in the hex is completely buried and its members killed. These traps are automatic.
                    3-4: Pitfalls. Pitfalls drop any unit in the hex three levels onto something unpleasant. This can be spikes, explosives, or
                    some corrosive substance. Apply 1D6 damage to all members of an affected unit. Surviving units take three turns to climb          NARRATIVE
Traps                                                                                                                                                 CAMPAIGNS
                    out from the pit or expend Jump Movement to exit in the next turn.
                    5-6: Firetraps. Firetraps are incendiary devices that cover all members of a unit in the hex with Inferno gel. Treat the unit
                    as though covered in the gel except that it lasts only until the End Phase on the next turn after the trap was activated.
                    Otherwise the effects are the same as for being struck by an Inferno missile.
                                                                                                                                                      MAP-BASED
                    When entering a hex, an Attacking unit may attempt to detect a trap. The Attacking player rolls 2D6. On a result of 9+,
                                                                                                                                                      CAMPAIGNS
                    any trap present was detected, and the Defending player must disclose the type of trap. Trap Detection rolls are made
                    immediately prior to entering the hex during the Movement Phase. Any unit that detects a trap may attempt to disarm it
                    per the Clearing Minefields rules (see p. 178, TO:AR). Disarming a trap requires occupying an adjacent hex and making no
                    weapon attacks. A disarming attempt can begin the same turn in which the trap was detected.                                      CHAOS
                    Huge explosions have left large pockets of hollow ground and hidden sinkholes. Whenever a unit fails a Piloting/Driving         CAMPAIGN
                    Skill roll while occupying a paved or rubble hex, roll 2D6 for a Sinkhole check. On a result of 8 or more, the pavement         CREATION
Unsteady Ground     weakens and a hole opens up (Sublevel 1) in the hex in which the failed Piloting Skill roll occurs. Because the unit already
                    failed its Piloting Skill Roll, the unit automatically falls; roll Falling Damage as normal. If the unit is a ground vehicle     MAINTENANCE,
                    skidding or sideslipping, it falls in the hex rather then skidding or sideslipping normally.                                       SALVAGE,
Wages of War        Use the Battlefield Wreckage rules (see p. 155, TO:AR).                                                                            REPAIR &
                                                                                                                                                    CUSTOMIZATION
Zellbrigen          Clan Honor rules are considered to be in effect for this track at Honor Level 1 (see p. 273, TW).

   While not required, the Situation section can help lay out the                   BUILDING

atmosphere and setting of the game. Here is where a GM provides
extraneous details such as the planetary system in which the battle
is taking place, or what the context is—perhaps it’s a localized
political feud, or the leading edge of a full-on faction assault—and       Ben is creating a campaign for his local players, who have
sets the “window dressing” of the coming conflict. It can also be a     decided to play a Periphery campaign as Clan Coyote warriors.
way for a GM to link the previous track to the current one, giving      After some research, he decides to insert the players into the 3103
an update of the universe around the players’ unit and addressing       invasion of Navarre in the Escorpión Imperio. Using other rules
any lingering effects or fallout from the prior mission.                in this book, he creates the Navarre system and sketches out a
   These fictional interludes can take form in a number of ways.        map of the world, designating six locations that will define the
They can be a simple, short narrative read aloud to the players, or     crux of the campaign.
a longer piece that is provided to each side for context. Or perhaps       Once the locations are selected, Ben decides on what kinds
they take the form of a series of news articles showcasing events or    of battles would occur at each location. He makes notes for
information that is relevant to the track’s primary objectives. Some    each one, as these will be helpful later in constructing the
more imaginative GMs may work up mock screencaps or video to            six tracks of the campaign. In addition, he chooses several
introduce the next section of the story. However done, the primary      generic Missions from Total Chaos that will help enhance
purpose of the Situation section is to set the tone and introduce       the invasion campaign.
the urgency of the mission that is about to be presented.                  Ben then settles down to construct one of the tracks, the initial

a 31st-century command combat-effective. The term “support
                                                                                             are tracking time across days, weeks, months and so on, then each
personnel” is used to describe all noncombatant members of a

                                                                                             the Maintenance/Repair Cycle.
   Maintenance: Even when not used in battle, the high-tech
weapons of the 31st century require constant maintenance to stay
  Overtime: Between campaigns a Technical Team can push to
work an additional 8 hours a day, but such efforts will provide
only 4 additional hours of productive work.
                                                                                                             UNIT QUALITY
                                                                                                          All units start with a Quality Rating based on their construction
SKILL CHECKS                                                                                          or (in the case of conventional infantry) training and equipment.
   Skill checks are dice rolls of 2D6 or 3D6—depending on                                                                                                                                 INTRODUCTION
                                                                                                      The Quality Rating Table (see below) gives an overview of the
the situation—made against a target number based on the                                               average Quality Rating players can expect in each BattleTech era.
Experience Rating of a Technical or Medical team (see Support                                         If so desired, players can use the more detailed Faction Quality
Personnel, p. 190), modified for various situations. Each type of                                     Table (see below). If players elect to use neither faction nor era                     FORCE
roll described in this section includes the name of the roll for                                      variations for unit quality, then all units start the campaign with                   CREATION
clarity, such as Maintenance Check, Replacement Check and                                             a Quality Rating of D (Average).
Repair Check for Technician Skill Checks, Wounded Check for a                                             Wear and tear, battle damage and maintenance cause a unit’s

  Description: A catch-all description of the Quality Rating.                                                                                                                              GENERATION
                                                                                                                                                                                          CONVERSIONS
  Cost Modifier: During a campaign the opportunity may arise for players to buy or sell units. The quality of the unit in question can adjust the price.

               PERSONNEL                                                   Clan Technology
                                                                              Because of their unfamiliarity with advanced Clan technology,
  A fighting unit depends on its support personnel to keep it              Inner Sphere technicians remain at a disadvantage when working
combat ready. The following rules assume an exceptionally well-            on Clan-built equipment (reflected on the appropriate tables with
supported force. At their discretion players may decide to use less        a negative modifier).
personnel and risk the consequences.
  Support personnel fall into two distinct categories: Technical           Technical Teams on the Battlefield
and Medical. Unless determined otherwise, support personnel                  If called for during a scenario, Technical Teams are represented
have a Regular Experience Rating. If they wish, players can use the        during play as individual 7-man rifle (ballistic) infantry squads
Random Experience Rating Table (see p. 273, TW) to determine the           with Gunnery and Anti-’Mech Skill Ratings listed on the Support
Experience Rating of each Technical and Medical team.                      Personnel Experience Table (see below).

TECHNICAL PERSONNEL                                                        Crew
   Techs (Technicians) and astechs (Assistant Technicians) are vital         WarShip, JumpShip, DropShip, Space Station, Large Naval
to the survival of any modern fighting force. Without them, repairs        Vessel Support Vehicle, and Mobile Structure crews provide
would be difficult and upgrades or modification impossible. Even
reloading ammunition presents the MechWarrior in the field with
a major challenge.                                                                                  SUPPORT PERSONNEL
   A full-strength Technical Team consists of one tech and six                                       EXPERIENCE TABLE
semi-skilled astechs. Such a team is commonly assigned to work
on a specific ’Mech (a BattleMech or IndustrialMech), Point of                                           Base Skill Target                       Combat Skills
ProtoMechs, a vehicle or a battle armor unit. A Technical Team                  Experience
                                                                                  Rating            Technical           Medical           Gunnery*           Anti-’Mech
assigned to support conventional infantry can support up to four
units. Technicians are classified by specific skills they possess, which           Green                 8+                10+                  7                 8/*
in turn dictate the type of unit they support. Additional unassigned             Regular                 6+                 8+                  7                 7/*
teams can be used to augment these when they are not deployed                    Veteran                 5+                 7+                  6                 6/*
on search and rescue or salvage operations. As a rule of thumb, a                   Elite                3+                 6+                  6                 5/*
player receives one unassigned team of his or her choice for every
                                                                              *A Medical Team can make attacks as though it is a foot (rifle, ballistic) squad; it cannot make
four assigned teams.                                                             Anti-’Mech attacks, however (see Medical Teams on the Battlefield, p. 191).
   Each type of technician can work on other types of units, but the
controlling player must roll 3D6 when making Technician Skill Checks
the necessary technical support for their units. They cannot
perform maintenance on other unit types, nor can they be
augmented with additional Technical Teams. Crew performing

maintenance uses the skill modifiers for their experience rating.
