     6+                                   Q-D                           Q-E                            Q-E                           Q-E                           Q-F                             *
Q–N: The unit Quality Rating changes to a new value, with N the new value as noted on the table. For example a MoF of 5 on a D Quality Rating unit would result in the unit downgrading to Quality Rating C,
    while a MoS of 5 on a Quality Rating B unit would result in the unit upgrading to Quality Rating C.
(X): Roll X times on the appropriate Damage Status Table, with X equal to the value on the table. For example a MoF of 5 on an A Quality Rating unit would result in 3 rolls on the appropriate Damage Status Table.
*Award 1 Experience Point, beyond any other Experience Points awarded during a scenario; a Tech Team may only be awarded 1 such extra Experience Point per Maintenance/Repair Cycle (see Experience, p. 211).

                                                                                                                                                    203
   The new personnel have no equipment of their own. For infantry,
the full cost of the appropriate unit type must be spent to outfit them.
Other personnel types can use what equipment is on hand or can
be bought for them. If the player chooses not to retain the services
of the generated personnel, he or she must roll again in the next
Maintenance/Repair Cycle.

REPAIRS AND REPLACEMENTS
  After obtaining the necessary parts, players can perform repairs,
replacements or partial repairs. The following rules describe the
process of making such repairs.
  All repairs require Technician Skill Checks. When performing
repairs and replacements, the conditions under which they
are performed will modify the target number, as shown on the
Maintenance, Repair and Salvage Check Modifiers Table (see p. 192).

Repair
   The term repair means returning a component to working order
without replacing it entirely. This requires a skilled technician and a
stock of tools and miscellaneous parts, but does not require specific
replacement parts. Repairs are cheaper than replacements, but are
more difficult to accomplish and less reliable.
   To repair a part, a Technical Team spends the time listed on the
Master Repair Table (see pp. 205-207) and then makes a Technician
Skill Check, modified as indicated on the Master Repair Table and the
Maintenance, Repair and Salvage Check Modifiers Table. If the roll
succeeds, the repair succeeds. If the roll fails, the component is still
damaged. (However, certain components may be partially repaired
on a failed Repair Check; see Partial Repairs, p. 208.) A Technical Team
may not attempt to repair the same part twice, nor may another
team of the same or lower Experience Rating attempt the job. Only
a Technical Team with a higher Experience Rating may attempt to
repair the part after a failed attempt. If an Elite Technical Team fails
to make the repair, it is impossible and the part must be replaced.
   Some equipment (such as Artemis IV FCS, machine-gun arrays
or an actuator enhancement system) modify the behavior of other
components. However, they must be repaired separately.

        Bob’s AS7-D Atlas has taken a beating fighting the Jade
     Falcons as they drive into the Lyran Alliance. It has suffered armor
     damage to every hit location and 7 points of internal structure
     damage to the left torso. In addition, the LRM-20 launcher has
     taken two critical hits (but passed the Diagnosis Check and can
     be repaired). The Veteran Technical Team has a Target Number
     of 6, while the Atlas has a Quality Rating of D (+0) and is being
     repaired in a ’Mech cubicle aboard a DropShip (+0).
        The armor cannot be repaired, only replaced. However, Bob
     can attempt to repair the LRM launcher and internal structure.
     The LRM launcher has a Tech Rating of C (+0) and has suffered
     two critical hits (–2). That gives a final modified Target Number of
     4 [6 (Veteran Skill Rating) + 0 (’Mech cubicle aboard a DropShip)
     + 0 (Quality Rating D) + 0 (Tech Rating C) – 2 (2 critical hits)
     = 4]. Bob rolls an 8, indicating that the LRM launcher is fully
     operational again after 150 minutes of work.

as external stores on fighters and some Fixed-Wing Support Vehicles.

        A Regular Technical Team is working in a ’Mech cubicle aboard           Each success returns one trooper to health, heals one hit suffered

     right torso. Each magazine will require 10 minutes to reload,

     while the first team reloads the LRM and SRM magazine in the            For campaign purposes, this takes 15 consecutive Maintenance/

     weapon is 15 minutes. Because the operation is being carried out             field (+2), the teams have a final modified Target Number of 9

JURY-RIGGING (OPTIONAL)
   Though replacement parts are not always readily available, any                   SUPPORT PERSONNEL EXP. TABLE
repair shop contains items such as scrap myomer, duct tape (lots
of duct tape), solder and other odds and ends. With these supplies,                     Old                    New             Complete Scenarios
a Technical Team can jury-rig a temporary bypass for the damaged                       Rating                 Rating               Required
or destroyed component until a more skilled team or the needed
                                                                                       Green                 Regular                    5
replacement part becomes available.
   To create a jury-rigged component, a team must make a                              Regular                Veteran                   10
successful Technician Skill Check with the appropriate TN modifiers                   Veteran                  Elite                   20
from the Maintenance, Repair, and Salvage Check Modifiers Table.
This quick fix is neither safe or durable. If using component Quality

     If they can complete one more scenario, they will meet the              There are six classes of customization, representing how
     requirement to advance to Regular Experience. They must              complex the work is. The procedures detailed in Classes D to F

      INTERNAL STRUCTURE DISTRIBUTION                                       Refit kits are standardized packages, carefully selected to
                   TABLE                                                 be compatible with a specific unit, and paired with extensive
                                                                         supporting documentation to make installation relatively
                                               Clan Endo Steel/IS &      straightforward.
        Hit Location         IS Endo Steel    Clan Endo-Composite           Refit kits are sourced like any other component, but are not
           Head                   1                    0                 available for DropShips, JumpShips, WarShips, or Space Stations.
                                                                         A kit’s cost is equal to the cost of its components plus 10 percent.
       Center Torso               1                    1
                                                                         The Availability Rating is equal to the highest Availability of the
        Side Torso                3                    1                 kit’s contents. The default Quality Rating is D, though other Ratings
           Arm                    2                    1                 are possible.
           Leg                    1                    1                    A refit kit halves the time multiplier of the refit the kit is meant
                                                                         to enable (do not round). It also applies a –2 skill check modifier
                                                                         to the task, and the automatic Quality Rating drop for performing
                                                                         work of Class C or higher is ignored. Lastly, a refit kit that includes
   Class D: Adding CASE or CASE II to a location that previously had     an engine drops the class for engine work from E to D.

of an existing turret.                                                   Refurbishment
                                                                            In spite of the best efforts of technical teams, a unit tends to
Factory Refits                                                           degrade over time. This process can be reversed via refurbishment.
  These refits are long and involved processes that can only be          Old parts are renovated or replaced, new or updated software
performed at the proper factory.                                         installed, and so on.

well as ejection and console additions/changes; treat as blown-          a ’Mech, aerospace fighter or Small Craft, a month for a DropShip
off head). Changing engine type and/or rating (unless done via a         or JumpShip, or three months for a WarShip or Space Station. The
refit kit; see below).                                                   cost is equal to 10 percent of the unit’s base value (not modified
   Class F: Adding a new turret. Changing internal structure type        for quality).

   Terrain Factor Rules: If players are using the Terrain Factor (see          The loss of personnel and equipment is only one way in which a
p. 63, TO:AR) and Battlefield Wreckage (see p. 155, TO:AR) rules,           force’s effectiveness can be destroyed. More insidious—and more
and a hex that contains a destroyed unit—now being tracked as               prevalent in campaigns—is the destruction of a force’s will to fight,
battlefield wreckage—is reduced, this reduction affects the salvage         usually brought about by reversals of fortune and poor conditions.
quality of the destroyed unit. Whenever such a hex is reduced (such         This occurs in two ways: morale and fatigue, the first principally
as from an ultra-rough hex to a rough hex), the Quality Rating of           mental and emotional and the latter physical.
any destroyed units in that hex are automatically reduced by 1                 The following rules show the larger scope of morale and fatigue
level (see Maintenance Check Table, p. 194). If tracking individual         across several scenarios, building on those concepts from Tactical
component ratings, all component ratings are likewise reduced by            Operations (see Morale, p. 179 and Fatigue, p. 166, TO:AR), where
1 level. Any components reduced below an A rating in this fashion           the rules focused directly during game play of a single scenario.
are considered destroyed, and if the unit’s overall Quality Rating             Terminology: In these rules, any time the term “company”
is reduced below A, it is classified as “truly destroyed” (see p. 197).     is used, it can mean a company, Trinary or Level III, or any other

SPECIAL CASES                                                               term “lance” can mean a lance, Platoon, Star, Level II, or any other
    The following special rules apply:                                      factional military organization of the same approximate size.

a Booby Trap (see p. 109, TO:AUE) during the battle. When salvaging                 Kim is trying to figure her Force Quality for a company with
equipment from a unit that contains an undetected and un-                        the following Piloting and Gunnery Skill Ratings:
triggered Booby Trap, there is a chance it will go off. Roll 2D6. On a
result of 11+, the Booby Trap is activated, destroying the salvaged                Pilot 1: 3/4
unit and all friendly support units participating in the operation.                Pilot 2: 4/6
      Units conducting salvage may sweep for Booby Traps prior to                  Pilot 3: 3/2
beginning salvage operations. This process takes 5 minutes, and                    Pilot 4: 5/4
runs the risk of setting off a Booby Trap on 11+, but will only destroy            Pilot 5: 2/2
the individual conducting the sweep. Found Booby Traps can be                      Pilot 6: 4/7
disarmed: this takes 30 minutes for a trained Explosive Ordnance                   Pilot 7: 1/2
Disposal (EOD) team, or 60 minutes for a general Tech Team. A                      Pilot 8: 5/5
dedicated EOD team automatically disarms the Booby Trap, but                       Pilot 9: 3/3
may not rush. An Elite Tech Team only triggers the Booby Trap on a                 Pilot 10: 2/1
12+, Veteran Teams on 11+, Regular 10+, and Green on 9+. Rushing                   Pilot 11: 4/4
the job applies a +1 modifier to the roll, while taking extra time                 Pilot 12: 0/2
subtracts 1.

Internal Bomb Bay (3 Points)
   The unit can use its internal cargo capacity as an internal
bomb bay, using one ton of cargo per bomb slot used. The
unit can release up to 6 bombs each turn (regardless of their
size). However, in the turn that ordnance is dropped, there is              With significant weight to throw behind its fists, the BattleMaster opts to
                                                                                        use a jettison-capable particle projection cannon.

reputation. As a result, it is worth only half the normal resale         points, but this version can only be purchased if the unit has
value, unless ’Mechs are particularly rare for a given era and/          more than one energy weapon.
or location (for example, in the late Third Succession War era,

their nose at even the most unpopular of machines).

one point in its career, but lose it later (such as the Battle Hawk).                                                                                 CREATION

on an Inner Sphere market.                                                  This quirk can be taken only once and only for a single

Gas Hog (2 Points)
  The unit is very inefficient and consumes fuel at twice the
normal rate when pushed above Cruising/Safe Thrust speed.
This quirk cannot be applied to fusion- or fission-powered
ground and naval units.
                                                                            INTRODUCTION
Hard to Pilot (2 Points)
  This unit is hard to operate and MechWarriors, pilots or crew
receive a +1 Target Number modifier to all Piloting/Driving Skill              FORCE
Rolls.                                                                        CREATION

Illegal Design (0 Points)
   Units designed with this quirk do not follow the existing
                                                                               FORCE
construction rules for some reason, achieving effects that
                                                                             OPERATIONS
are not normally allowed in game-play, and which can fail
spectacularly at any moment. (Note: The total weight and
                                 critical space of all components
                                    must still fit within the design’s       FORMATION
                                     tonnage and critical space.)             BUILDING
                                       If employed, the opposing
                                    player may roll 2D6 to determine
                                                                            INNER SPHERE
                                 if the unit with this quirk suffers a
                                                                               AT WAR
catastrophic failure once every 6 turns of combat. On a result of           CONVERSIONS
6 or higher, such catastrophic failure occurs in 1D6 of the unit’s
hit locations (using the Front/Back Hit Locations Table). A Critical        INNER SPHERE
Hit check is then made for each failure location determined,                SOLAR SYSTEM
                                                                               AT WAR
applying a +4 modifier to the roll result. On any modified roll              GENERATION
                                                                            CONVERSIONS
result of 13+, the location suffers complete collapse if it is an
arm, leg, head, wing, main gun, or turret. This will destroy all
items within (and any explosive components checks should be                    LINKED
made as appropriate for location destruction). If the affected               SCENARIOS
location is none of the above, it suffers 4 critical hits, determined
as normal.
   Illegal units suffer an additional +4 Target Number modifier to
                                                                             NARRATIVE
repair or replace any damaged or destroyed components, and
                                                                             CAMPAIGNS
double all repair and maintenance times for work performed
on them. If an Illegal unit repair fails, the item gains the Non-
Functional quirk as well.
   Finally, because they are technically failures at the time of their       MAP-BASED
production, Illegal units should also receive the Obsolete quirk             CAMPAIGNS
(see p. 234), with the year of their obsolescence determined to
be the same as their year of introduction.
                                                                               CHAOS
                                                                              CAMPAIGN
Inaccurate Weapon (Varies)                                                    CREATION

receives a +1 Target Number modifier. The value is 1 point per            MAINTENANCE,
5 points (or fraction thereof) of maximum damage the weapon                 SALVAGE,
or bay can inflict in a single Damage Value grouping. More than             REPAIR &
one weapon or bay on a unit can receive this negative quirk.             CUSTOMIZATION

   This DropShip is so big that it takes up two docking collars                SHEETS
instead of one, as in the case of the Behemoth.

as the Dark Crow—have mounted their arms far too low to take

                                                                            FAILED ADMINISTRATION TABLE
                    Failed
                     Roll        Result
                       2-6       No significant event. Combatants make negative jokes about REMF competency, hurting administrators’ feelings.
                                 Poor legal or human resource paperwork handling results in a financial loss as the Force scrambles to cover missed paydays,
                       7-8
                                 harassment lawsuits, workplace injuries, etc. The Force loses 1D6 x 10 precent of a month of peacetime operating costs.
                                 Lesson planner scrambles training schedule for a quarter before anyone notices how easy training has gotten. All units in the
                        9
                                 Force make a Skill Rating loss roll as if they had missed a quarter of training, as discussed under Training, page 51.
                                 Understaffed intelligence group means the Force is ill-prepared for the next battle. The Force suffers a –2 Initiative modifier
                        10
                                 throughout the next battle.
                                 Quartermaster gambles spare parts budget on sure-thing investment in recovered Star League myomer lubricant (made
                        11       with snake oil). All units in the Force must make a breakdown roll as if they had gone without spare parts for a month, per
                                 Maintenance, above.
                                 Legal officer misreads contract / orders and local laws, resulting in Force being charged with a Mission Failure. Refer to Mission
                        12
                                 Failure (see p. 47) for Reputation and budget penalties. Pirate Forces treat this result as a 10.

                                                                                                                                                            P. 57
                                                               UNIT ROLES TABLE
                                                                     GROUND UNIT ROLES
Name                                                                         Brief Description                                                Page Reference
Ambusher                  Slow, lightly armored, prefer short-range engagements; cheap and easy to deploy                                          57
Brawler                   Adequate speed, adequate armor, able to do damage at range and even more as they close                                   57
Juggernaut                Slow, very tough, preferring close combat; excellent at holding the line, not at running battles                         57
Missile Boat              Majority of long-range damage comes from indirect fire-capable weapons                                                   58
Scout                     Fast, lightly armored and very little weaponry; avoids battles, provides spotting and support                            58
Skirmisher                Fast with adequate or heavy armor but lighter weaponry; great for shifting battles                                       58
Sniper                    Sit still and fire; defense may come from range, stealth, armor or cover                                                 58
Striker                   Fast, light armor, short- or medium-range weapons; speed is life                                                         58


                                                                    AEROSPACE UNIT ROLES
Name                                                                         Brief Description                                                Page Reference
Attack Fighter            Slow but heavily armored and armed; often lacking in long-range firepower                                                58
Dogfighter                Medium or heavy fighters; direct fighting with other fighters                                                            58
Fast Dogfighter           Light or medium fighters; intercept any fighters that breach initial dog-fight lines                                     58
Fire Support              Medium or heavy fighters; mostly long-range weapons to provide air or ground support                                     58
Interceptor               Light, fast fighters for engagement as far from defensive perimeters as possible                                         58
Transport                 Generally not fighters; small craft, DropShips and support vehicles for transport                                        58

                                                SPECIAL PILOT ABILITIES
Ability                    Cost   Brief Description                                                                                  Page Reference
Animal Mimicry              2     Unit gains mobility bonus and ability to demoralize opponents                                           72
Antagonizer                 3     Unit can enrage an opponent for a brief period                                                          73
Blood Stalker               2     Unit may better focus its attacks on a preferred target                                                 73
Cluster Hitter              2     Unit can deliver extra damage in an attack using missiles or flak weapons                               73
Combat Intuition            3     Unit may move and resolve fire before any other unit acts                                               73
Cross-Country               2     Ground vehicle unit may enter some illegal terrain types, but at high Move cost                         73
Demoralizer                 3     Unit can intimidate an opponent for a brief period                                                      74
Dodge                       2     Unit can attempt to evade physical attacks                                                              74
Eagle’s Eyes                2     Unit gains (or augments) its ability to spot hidden units and avoid mines                               74
Environmental Specialist    2     Reduces movement and combat modifiers in a preferred environment                                        74
Fist Fire                   2     Unit delivers extra damage in physical attacks                                                          75
Forward Observer            1     Unit improves accuracy of indirect fire when used as a spotter                                          75
Heavy Lifter                1     Enables increased carrying capacity                                                                     76
Hopper                      1     Unit may attempt Piloting Skill Roll when leg blown off; may not be reduced below 2 MP                  76
Hot Dog                     2     Helps avoid overheating effects                                                                         76
Human TRO                   1     Increases likelihood of critical hits                                                                   76
Iron Will                   2     Unit can resist psychological attacks and receives a bonus during Morale Checks                         76
Jumping Jack                2     Improves accuracy of any attack made when the unit jumps                                                76




                                                                                                                                                          FORMATION BUILDING
Lucky                      1-4    Unit may reroll a limited number of failed attacks and Piloting Skill/Control Rolls per scenario        77
Maneuvering Ace             2     Enables or improves lateral shift movement                                                              77
Marksman                    2     Unit may make an Aimed Shot                                                                             77
Melee Master                2     Unit can deliver an additional physical attack                                                          77
Melee Specialist            1     Unit delivers physical attacks with greater accuracy                                                    77
Multi-Tasker                2     Reduces penalty modifiers for attacks against multiple targets                                          78
Natural Grace               3     Reduces MP costs through certain terrain, enables improved movement abilities                           78
Oblique Artilleryman        1     Improves accuracy and reduces scatter for all artillery weapon attacks                                  78
Oblique Attacker            1     Improves accuracy for indirect fire, and enables indirect attacks without a spotter                     78
Range Master                2     Unit gains bonus to attacks at preferred range                                                          78
Sandblaster                 2     Unit improves accuracy and damage when only using AC and missile weapons                                79
Sharpshooter                4     Unit may make an Aimed Shot with additional chance for critical hit                                     79
Slugger                     1     Unit may fire weapon while using an improvised club                                                     80
Sniper                      3     Unit reduces Medium, Long, and Extreme range modifiers by half                                          80
Speed Demon                 2     Unit can move faster than normal                                                                        80
Stand-Aside                 1     Unit can pass directly through enemy units with successful roll                                         80
Street Fighter              2     Unit may pre-empt an attack against it by enemies in base contact                                       80
Swordsman                   2     Unit can deliver an Aimed Shot or improved damage in physical weapon attack                             80
Tactical Genius             3     Enables command unit to reroll Initiatives                                                              80
Terrain Master                                                                                                                            81

   Forest Ranger            3     Unit moves more easily through (and gains extra cover from) woods and jungle                            81
   Frogman                  3     Unit moves more easily than others while fully submerged                                                81
   Mountaineer              3     Unit moves more easily through Level changes and rough terrain types                                    81
   Nightwalker              3     Unit ignores modifiers for darkness                                                                     81
   Swamp Beast              3     Unit moves more easily through mud and swamp terrain; ignores bog down in same                          81
Weapon Specialist           3     Unit can deliver a more accurate attack with preferred weapon                                           82
Zweihander                  2     ’Mech unit delivers more damage in physical attacks                                                     82

*See Force Quality, page 216. †Including crew of Support Vehicles ‡ See Force Loyalty, page 217.
§The time frame for these two modifiers is based on the Force Quality, with the following values representing a number of Morale/Fatigue Cycles: Elite = 1; Veterans = 2; Regular = 3; Green = 4. For example, for an
   Elite force, these modifiers are only applied if the force suffered a desertion/mutineer in the previous Morale/Fatigue Cycles; if it suffered a desertion/mutineer two Morale/Fatigue Cycles in the past, the modifiers
   do not apply. A Green force, however, must have four Morale/Fatigue Cycles pass without a desertion/mutineer for these modifiers to no longer apply for a new Morale/Fatigue Cycle.
